SOL.courses: The LLB program is a five years regular program (six years in the extension program) that studies diverse areas of law to achieve the following: 1. a) provide thorough education in law to produce well-rounded and competent graduates who are well versed in the knowledge of domestic and international laws, equipped with skills for successful practice of law, and committed to serve the causes of justice; 2. b) support the developmental and democratization process in the country; 3. c) examine interfaces and interlinkages among indigenous legal knowledges and philosophies of Ethiopia and their implication on formal legal system; 4. d) enhance original and critical thinking in legal education; 5. e) foster multiculturalism in legal education and scholarship; 6. f) promote civic and community engagement; 7. g) promote prevalence of rule of law, human rights and social justice through legal education and research; and 8. h) equip students with the ethics and responsibilities of the legal profession in the administration of justice. Students who are admitted to the program will be engaged in in-depth theoretical, practical, national, international and comparative studies in the fields of law. Admission to the program will be made on competitive basis from among applicants who have completed the first Semester of the freshman year at Addis Ababa University. The eligibility criteria for admission to the regular and continuing LL.B. Program, as well as advanced standing, are as follows: 1. i) The candidate must have scored 50% or above in the national higher education entrance examination, 2. ii) The candidate should meet a minimum GPA in the first semester of the freshman program of the university (3.00 for male and 2.75 for female, disadvantaged and unrepresented applicants), and iii) The candidate must pass the law school entrance exam. The weight assigned for the admission requirements is as follows: 1. i) National higher education entrance exam .... 20% 2. ii) First semester freshman program result ....50% iii) Law school entrance exam .... 30% 1. iv) Affirmative action .... 5%

SOL.department_name: Master of Laws (LL.M) in Public International Law
SOL.courses: LL.M in Public International Law The LL.M in Public International Law is a one-and-a-half-year regular program (three years in the extension program) that studies diverse areas of public international law to build the knowledge, research, consultancy and teaching capabilities of the students in current international law matters. Students who had LLB degree will be engaged in in-depth studies in the fields of public international law to enhance their capacities to analyze fundamental issues and provide advice and opinion on current international law matters, conduct research, and represent their states and governments in the various regional and international bodies, including in international litigation. The courses to be covered include: Research Methods in Law and Social Science; Law of Treaties; Law of International Organizations; Settlement of International Disputes; Theories of International Relations; elective courses; and LL.M Thesis. The elective courses will be chosen from the following: African Union Law; International Criminal Law; International Economic Law; International Environmental Law; International Human Rights Law; International Water Law; or The Law of the Sea. Admission to the program is limited to applicants who have first degree in law (LLB) and based on Graduate Admission Test (GAT) of the Addis Ababa University, and LSAT. and entrance examination of the School of Law

SOL.department_name: Master of Laws (LL.M) in Criminal Justice
SOL.courses: The LL.M in Criminal Justice is a one-and-a-half-year regular program (three years in the extension program) that studies criminal law making and administration of justice to build the knowledge, research, consultancy and teaching capabilities of the students in the fields of criminal law making and administration of justice. Students who had LLB degree will be engaged in in-depth studies in the fields of criminal law making and administration of justice to enhance their capacities to analyze fundamental issues and contribute to the design and implementation of the ongoing policies and reform measures of the country in the administration of criminal justice. The courses to be covered include: Research Methods in Law and Social Science; Advanced Legal Theory; Human Rights in Criminal Justice; Constitutional issues in Criminal Litigation; Transitional Justice; Criminalization and Criminal Law Making; Contemporary Issues in Criminal Justice; International Criminal Law; Rule of Law in Criminal Justice; elective courses; and LL.M. Thesis. The elective courses will be chosen from the following: Comparative Criminal Justice; Customary Criminal Justice; Sentencing and Post-Conviction Remedies; or Theories and Ideologies in Criminal Justice. Admission to the program is limited to applicants who have first degree in law (LLB) and based on Graduate Admission Test (GAT) of the Addis Ababa University, and LSAT.

SOL.department_name: Master of Laws (LL.M) in Business and Property Law
SOL.courses: LL. M in Business and Property Law The LL.M in Business and Property Law is a one-and-a-half-year regular program (three years in the extension program) that studies diverse areas of business and property law to build the knowledge, research, consultancy and teaching capabilities of the students in the fields of business and property law. The learners will be engaged in in-depth studies in the fields of business and property law to enhance their capacities to contribute to the design and implementation of the ongoing policies and reform measures of the country, including the implementation of a free market economic system, decentralization of governance, and building the capacities of newly established institutions. The courses to be covered include: Research Methods in Law and Social Science; Theories, Principles and Practices of Market Regulation, Competition and Consumer Protection; Advanced Issues in Company Law; Advanced Issues in Property Law; Law of Natural Resources; Land Policy and Law; Advanced Intellectual Property Law; E-Commerce Law; Laws of Banking, Microfinance and Insurance Markets; Laws of Capital and Securities Markets; International Economic Law; International Commercial Law; Commercial Dispute Resolution; Law of Taxation of Business and Property; Business and Human Rights; an elective course; and LL.M Thesis. The elective course will be chosen from the following: Advanced Issues in Investment Law; Construction and Construction Contracts Law; Transport and Transport Contracts Law; Advanced Issues in Environmental Law; Advanced Issues in Land Use and Lease Law; Housing and Real Estate Law; Law of Urban Property and Land Registration; or Law of Urban Property and Land Dispute Resolution. Admission to the program is limited to applicants who have first degree in law (LLB) and will be based on Graduate Admission Test (GAT) of the Addis Ababa University, and Law School Admission Test (LSAT)

The PhD in Law is a four years regular program that investigates a particular problem to build the knowledge, research, consultancy and teaching capabilities of the student in law. After course work, students will be engaged in an in-depth study of a particular area of law to carry out high level, problem-solving legal research that contribute to the advancement of the rule of law and socio-economic development in the country. The courses to be covered include: Advanced Research Methodology; Sociology of Law; Law and Economics; Law and Globalization; Theories, Processes and Structures of Constitutional Governance; Economic Regulation; Selected Books Seminar; Seminar on the Administration of Justice in Ethiopia; and PhD Dissertation. Admission to the program is limited to applicants who have first and second degrees in law (LLB and LLM) or first degree in law (LLB) and MA in interdisciplinary legal Studies from an accredited university and based on Graduate Admission Test (GAT) of the Addis Ababa University and entrance examination and screening requirements of the School of Law. Applicants must have a positively assessed undergraduate or graduate thesis or, for an applicant without a graduate thesis, submit a peer reviewed publication; pass a competency-based interview to be conducted by an Admission Committee to be established by the GC of the School of Law; submit at least two reference letters from previous teachers, research supervisors or thesis examiners; file a concept note that convincingly describes a potential research area; and produce a solid motivation letter. Program Title: Executive Masters in Managing Peace and Security in Afric

CHS.courses: The BSc in Medical Laboratory Science is a 4 year program that teaches students the basic principles of laboratory sciences and their monumental roles in healthcare. The program includes lessons in basic immunology, serology, hematology, immunohematology, molecular biology, clinical chemistry, urinalysis, bacteriology, parasitology, mycology, virology, public health microbiology and histopathology to name a few. The program provides three clinical laboratory attachments and laboratory internship that are designed to increase the students’ practical skills by exposing them to the routine laboratory practices at health centers and federal teaching hospitals. The program includes community based trainings to improve health issues in the community by developing the students’ problem identifying and solving skills. Students are expected to produce a research project by the end of the program with the help of their well-seasoned advisors. The program is also complete with general as well as supportive courses such as epidemiology, biostatistics, research methodology, economics, entrepreneurship; critical thinking, health service management that will enhance the students’ understanding of the medical laboratory sciences and provides them with wholesome knowledge, skills and experience to succeed after graduation in addition to helping them standout in the job market. The program aims to produce graduates capable of performing and reporting medical laboratory tests by following standard operating procedures with integrity and highest level of professionalism. In addition, the program’s objectives are to produce medical laboratory professionals with great teamwork spirit, creative problem solving skills and digital literacy

four years, and combines theoretical learning with hands-on clinical experience in various healthcare settings, such as hospitals, maternity wards, and health centers. Under the supervision of experienced midwives, they assist in prenatal visits, deliveries, and postpartum care. It is a rewarding career path for those passionate about maternal and child health, offering opportunities to make a meaningful impact on the lives of families. Graduates of a BSc in Midwifery are qualified to work as registered midwives, providing care to women and newborns in various settings. Midwives play a critical role in promoting safe and positive childbirth experiences. They provide care that respects the dignity, privacy, and cultural needs of women, and they are advocates for normal, physiological birth processes. Midwives also offer health education, counseling, and support to women and families throughout their reproductive lives Admission requirement for the program: Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE.

The Bachelor of Pharmacy is a five-year regular program that covers the fundamental principles of the science and practice involved in discovering, producing, preparing, dispensing, reviewing, and monitoring medications aiming to ensure the safe, effective, and affordable use of medicines. Students will develop a strong foundation in the knowledge, skills, attitudes, and behaviors necessary to support the healthcare system and meet patient needs. Graduates will be prepared to work in hospital and community pharmacies, drug manufacturing, import and wholesale companies, public health services, academic institutions, pharmaceutical research, drug regulatory bodies, and quality control organizations.

Specialty diploma in pathology is a three-year post graduate residency program in the college of health sciences. It is a hands-on training program that gives supervised clinical practice in advanced anatomic pathology. Trainees go through in-depth clinical rotations on the major areas of surgical Pathology, cytopathology, hematopathology and Autopsy. Admission requirements for the program are an M.D degree from a recognized training program, valid and updated registration with the respective authority of the Federal Ministry of Health (FM HACA), government or private sponsorship letter according to the policy of the ministry of health or the NRMP, at least one year of service/practice as a physician (GP or above level), application letters from the candidates and two supportive letters of reference (recommendation) from supervisors and/or professional colleagues. In addition, applicants should have taken and passed the graduate admission test (GAT) offered by the university.

CHS.department_name: Specialty in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery
CHS.courses: Specialty Certificate in Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery( ??? ???? ????:????? ?? ???? ??????) is a four year specialty regular program that studies Diseases, of Head and Neck region,Oro-facial tumors, craniofacial trauma, Developmental Abnormalities of the face such as Cleft Lip and Palate,Abnormal growth of the Jaws and their Surgical correction, Fractures of the Facial bones and their management, Soft tissue abnormalities and trauma of the face with their corrective surgery, and all abnormalities and advanced lesions of Oral and Craniofacial Region. The Residents will participate on Minor Oral Surgery cases management, teaching dental interns, Teaching medical interns and clinical students, Community outreach, Team training, Management of Major Maxillofacial cases surgically. They will also have an attachment at related departments such as General Surgery, Emergency Medicine,ENT, Plastic Surgery, Internal Medicine, Neurosurgery, Ophthalmology, Orthopedic Surgery, Anesthesiology and Critical care. The residents will also perform and be exposed to advanced reconstructive surgeries of the Head and Neck region. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of Graduate Admission Test (GAT) and Department Level Entrance Examination. Applicants must be Holders of DMD (Doctor of Dental Medicine) Degree and Equivalent from recognized higher Education.

Doctor of Dental Medicine or Doctor of Medicine in Dentistry(DMD) is a six and half year regular program that studies diseases, abnormalities, infirmities and deviations from normal of oral and oro-facial structures and their management. Students study premedical modules and courses for the first one year, which are common for Natural Science students and then deal with the Biomedical Sciences including all modules of Preclinical Medicine with students of the Medical Doctor Program for two years. In the fourth year, the students will start clinical Dentistry and deal with the management of Dental, oral and Maxillofacial Diseases, trauma, tumors and developmental abnormalities as well as Medical problems associated with oral manifestation. They also study about Medical problems which determine dental and maxillofacial treatments. After they deal with clinical dentistry which include theoretical, skill based demonstrations and clinical exposure for two and half years, they will sit for qualifying examinations. After passing the final qualifying exam successfully, the students will join an internship program of one year duration. During the final(internship) period, the interns(Candidate Doctors of Dental Medicine) will have a chance to full exposure to the dental clinical practice which include Management of pulpal and periodontal diseases, restoring lost dental structures by prosthesis, filling of teeth, Managing surgical cases such as dento-alveolar fracture management, Attending Minor Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery sessions, Assisting Oral and Maxillofacial Surgery Specialists in the Major Operation, Performing removable and fixed Orthodontic treatments Descriptive research and engaged in community outreach under supervision. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination(EHEEECE),the choice of the student and the capacity of the Department/Center/School as well as UGAT(Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. In addition, the department will prepare Dental entrance Examination for the students who have completed first semester of General modules in Natural Science. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE

CHS.department_name: Specialty in Anesthesiology Critical Care and Pain Medicine
CHS.courses: There are 26 modules in this program. The first-year modules are a prerequisite for all other clinical modules in the consecutive two years. Some modules are shorter and given once, some are delivered repeatedly in different years (Thoracic, Neuro-Anesthesia, and Critical Care will be given in year II and III). The modules delivered in different years deal with competencies in different depth and with repeated exposures, the trainee will eventually master these skills. Research will be given longitudinally starting from the second year and completing in the end of the third year.

The Bachelor of Science (BSc) program in Anesthesia is four and Half (4.5) years regular program designed to meet the growing demand for skilled anesthesia professionals in both global and national healthcare landscapes, where advancements are continually improving patient outcomes and surgical success rates .The program studies the knowledge, skill, and attitude required to take a comprehensive history of the patient , perform a relevant physical examination, order and interpret basic laboratory and diagnostic modalities, stratify risk based on these findings, obtain informed consent, various Airway management modalities , optimize and prepare patients for anesthesia ., safe administration of anesthetic agents, understanding of complex physiological responses ,critical and emergency care , pain management techniques, and patient monitoring technologies

The Adult Cardiology Fellowship is a two-year program designed to develop highly competent and ethical medical practitioners that are also leaders of change in cardiovascular medicine. Fellows will gain experience to provide leadership in establishing and running new cardiology services, carryout research to define national cardiovascular problems and propose new models of control and prevention as well as provision of cost-effective management. They will work alongside experienced Cardiologists and gain practical experience in patient care, research, and clinical procedures. They will Benefit from access to modern diagnostic tools to strong research facilities

The Clinical Oncology speciality Program is a post-graduate, four-year clinical training program that trains general practitioners (GPs) in the field of Clinical Oncology. The field encompasses Medical Oncology and Radiation Oncology embracing the non-surgical management of malignant disease. The training program aims to enable the trainees to have the skills, attitudes, and knowledge required to be full-fledged clinical oncologists who can run out-patient clinical oncology clinics, manage hospitalized patients with oncology problems, perform appropriate procedures independently, develop and run vertical and horizontal health programs, conduct relevant researches, rationally utilize available resources, participate in health promotion and preventive activities concerning cancer-related problems.

We build an ongoing foundation in nuclear medicine, and emphasize how this discipline serves varied communities to facilitate in diagnosis and treatment of different disease. Residents learn to take a scientific perspective as they critically evaluate and treat patients. By valuing the principles of nuclear medicine, our residents obtain the critical and creative edge to innovate and be relevant in an evolving nuclear medicine. Built upon the fundamentals of medicine, our curriculum ensures that residents can cover deep scientific understanding of nuclear medicine, prpoer evaluation of patients, safe way of treating individuals and protecting the environment. Our residents are informed and curious about patient's need, and develop a critical understanding of nuclear medicine that allows them to identify the proper patients. By honing methods of investigative research, relationship building and interviewing, our residents learn to gather the evidences needed to craft compelling narratives. Their creative works are refined through individual mentoring from instructors as well as through intensive resident peer review. The program’s faculty are innovative teachers and professionals who engage residents in their current residency through hands-on experience and personalized mentoring. Residents evaluate patients, investigate them, plan for further measures; Present each case with the consultatnts and learn for corporate decision. The residents will do research; present teaching sessions and participate in update lectures. Our goal is to train residents to be savvy nuclear medicine practioners who can succeed not only in the realm of medicine in Ethiopia, but also in other parts of the world

Post-graduate Urology specialty Surgery Program is a comprehensive five-year program designed for medical professionals aiming to acquire advanced expertise in Urology care. The Program provides in-depth training in various aspects of general and urology surgery, including preoperative assessment, operative techniques, and postoperative care of Urology patients. It offers an in-depth foundation on basic principles of surgery and a wide experience in the diagnosis and treatment of a wide range of urology conditions, enhancing their attitude and skills in patient care, surgical decision-making, ethics, and urological procedures. During their study, trainees will acquire the knowledge and skill to urology diseases and eventually will be capable of operating independently on common emergency and elective urology cases. The program also offers exposure to radiology, Anesthesia, Orthopedics, Obstetrics/gynecology, nephrology and pediatric surgery. Trainees render services in outpatient and in patient departments both during regular working days and duty hours. They are also expected to do research which they would present towards the end of the training. Selection of applicants is the responsibility of Addis Ababa University, College of Health Sciences, School of Medicine, Department of Surgery, Urology division. MD degree is a must with preferences given to those with work experiences. Applicants must also pass an entrance exam and Graduate Admission test (GAT) which will be provided by the university.

CHS.department_name: Specialty in ENT (Otorhinolaryngology Head and Neck Surgery)
CHS.courses: ENT Program Description Speciality in Otorhinolaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery is a rigorous four-year program focused on the medical and surgical management of disorders related to the ear, nose, throat (ENT), and related structures of the head and neck. It covers a wide range of topics including anatomy, pathology, audiology, and speech-language pathology, with an emphasis on both clinical and surgical skills. Students start with foundational knowledge in medical sciences and gradually progress to more specialized areas, such as head and neck oncology, rhinology, laryngology, and otology. Throughout the program, students engage in hands-on clinical practice, honing their diagnostic and therapeutic skills in real-world settings. As they advance, they participate in surgical rotations, gaining experience in various ENT procedures under the supervision of attending surgeons. Admission to the Otolrhinoaryngology, Head and Neck Surgery program is based on the results of the University Entrance Exam, the applicant’s interest in the field, and the capacity of the department. The applicant should be holder of Degree of Medicine from recognized Unversity or College.He/she should have General parishioners license, younger than 45 years old with no serious health condtions..Foreign applicants must hold a degree of Doctor of Medicine, must produce their Professional license, and a letter of good standing from their local accreditation .

CHS.department_name: Specialty in Plastic and Reconstructive Surgery
CHS.courses: Plastic and reconstructive surgery is a subspecialty of surgery dedicated to care for all human beings and is not limited by sex, age anatomic area. It is broadly defined as a specialty/surgical practice that deals with the correction of deformities, malformations, malfunction, and reconstruction of defects resulting from different causes. Eg Burn, Trauma, after tumor excision in born lesions, etc

The Neurosurgical Specialty Training Program is a comprehensive five-year clinical residency designed to cultivate physicians proficient in managing neurological conditions requiring surgical intervention. The program is structured into three progressive levels, beginning with core neurosciences and advancing through basic to sophisticated neurosurgical courses. Trainees will actively engage in clinical activities, honing the essential skills and knowledge necessary for their progression. By the conclusion of the program, trainees are expected to be fully capable of evaluating patients, deliberating management options, and successfully performing surgical interventions for a wide range of neurosurgical conditions. Candidates must successfully complete the Ethiopian Residency Matching Program administered by the Federal Ministry of Health (FMOH) and have a minimum of one year of clinical experience as a general practitioner. Further eligibility evaluations will be conducted by the Neurosurgical Division of Surgery Department, Addis Ababa University. Preference will be given to those demonstrating a long-term commitment to addressing the human-resource needs of Ethiopia.

The specialty in thoracic surgery is a five-year regular clinical training program that provides advanced training in the comprehensive and multidisciplinary management of diseases of the lung, trachea, foregut, chest wall and mediastinum. The thoracic surgery resident will be involved in every aspect of care as patients move through the healthcare system, including outpatient evaluation in two different settings; arranging for evaluation services such as laboratory and radiology services; decisions regarding least costly yet medically appropriate care for patients; explanation of therapy alternatives to patients and families, making clear the risk-benefit ratio for the individual patient; working with fellow healthcare providers in other specialties to deliver high quality, safe patient care as a multidisciplinary team; and arranging for discharge when ready to an appropriate facility.  Trainee selection will be the responsibility of the AAU-CHS, School of Medicine, department of surgery, cardiothoracic surgery unit. Preference will be given to applicants who show excellent performances on their graduate program and a long term commitment to serve the humanresource needs of Ethiopia and those who have practice and development support (sponsorship) in hand specialty from teaching medical institutions training surgeons in government institutions. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education and Ministry of Health for their equivalency.

The General Surgery Specialty Certificate Program is a comprehensive four-year specialty program designed for medical professionals aiming to acquire advanced expertise in surgical care. The program provides in-depth training in various aspects of general surgery, including preoperative assessment, operative techniques, and postoperative management. Trainees will gain hands-on experience in diagnosing and treating a wide range of surgical conditions, enhancing their skills in patient management, surgical decision-making, and advanced surgical procedures

The Clinical Radiology Specialty Program at Addis Ababa University is an advanced and comprehensive three-and-a-half-year graduate program designed to equip trainees with the necessary skills and knowledge in the field of radiology. It begins with a six-month foundation in basic radiology principles, followed by intensive training in clinical radiology applications, such as CT, MRI, and ultrasound imaging. The curriculum is structured to cover various subspecialties, including musculoskeletal, thoracic, cardiac, neuroradiology, and pediatric radiology, among others. The program emphasizes a blend of theoretical learning and practical, hands-on experience in a modular fashion to ensure that graduates are proficient and ready to provide high-quality diagnostic services. Trainees are also encouraged to engage in research, thereby contributing to the advancement of the field. The program's mission aligns with the broader institutional goals of fostering a comprehensive understanding of both local health needs and global radiology standards

Medical Radiologic Technology Program Description The BSc in Medical Radiologic Technology is a four-year regular program that covers diagnostic radiological imaging principles and applications, including radiography, mammography, CT-Scan, MRI, and diagnostic ultrasound in the diagnosis of human disease. Students will study human basic sciences, clinical sciences (internal medicine, surgery, gynecology & obstetrics, and pediatrics), diagnostic radiological imaging courses (radiological physics and equipment, radiological imaging, radiological positioning, application of CT scan and MRI, abdominal and pelvic ultrasound, gynecology and obstetrics ultrasound, and radiological image interpretation), and public health courses. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of medical radiologic technology department as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE

CHS.department_name: Specialty in Pediatric and Child Health
CHS.courses: The specialty program in Pediatrics and Child Health is a three-year regular program that is designed to equip physicians with the knowledge and skills necessary to provide comprehensive care for infants, children, and adolescents. This program emphasizes a holistic approach to child health, addressing the physical, emotional, and developmental needs of children while promoting their health and well-being across diverse populations. Admission requirement for the program are in accordance with the eligibility criteria set by the Federal Ministry of Education for higher education. Applicants should also pass the residency entrance examination and the General Aptitude Test (GAT) offered by the university. Candidates could have government sponsorship or should be able to self-fund their residency program. Additionally, candidates should be registered and licensed to practice medicine by the relevant authority in Ethiopia

CHS.department_name: Specialty in Orthopedics and Trauma Surgery
CHS.courses: The Specialty Training in Orthopaedics and Trauma Surgery is a four-year regular program that studies management of wide verities of Orthopaedic diseases and conditions that involve the Musculoskeletal system. It includes prevention of Orthopaedic diseases, non-surgical and surgical treatment, rehabilitation and basic Orthopaedic research. Admission requirement for the program is based on pass results of the entrance examinations administered by Ethiopian National Residency Matching Program(ENRMP), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as GAT (Graduate Admission Test) offered by our University. Applicants from foreign countries will be selected based on the collaborated criteria set by the University and Ministry of Health. Finally, the Department runs its own physical examination and eye examination by ophthalmologists to assess the fitness of the candidate. After these necessary steps, the candidate will be accepted to register into the program.

CHS.department_name: Specialty in Emergency and Critical Care Medicine
CHS.courses: The Emergency and Critical Care Medicine Residency Program at Addis Ababa University is a three-year training designed to equip medical graduates with advanced skills in managing acutely ill patients across emergency departments and ICUs. Established in response to Ethiopia's growing need for skilled professionals in this field, the program aligns with the university's mission and national healthcare goals.

Speciality Certificate in Ophthalmology The Specialty Certificate in Ophthalmology is a comprehensive four-year program designed to provide in-depth knowledge and training on the structure, functions, and diseases of the eye. This program aims to prepare healthcare professionals for a successful career in ophthalmology. Admission requirement for the program: ? The candidate should be a holder of a degree of Doctor of Medicine from a recognized university/college. ? The candidate must be a registered general practitioner by the Federal Ministry of Health of Ethiopia. ? The candidate must pass the national entrance written examination and interview. ? Foreign applicants must hold a degree of Doctor of Medicine, must produce their professional license, and a letter of good standing from their local accreditation body. ? Admission of foreign applicants shall be governed by the legislation of the University/College. ? Applicants must be in acceptable physical and mental health, without any serious health conditions that would limit their ability to cope with the requirements of the training program and service. ? Applicant would undergo ophthalmic examination for fitness (Visual Acuity, color vision test, stereopsis, alignment tests and slit-lamp examination) ? The department reserves the right to deny admission to unfit candidates (after medical checkup) as determined by the department council. ? Applicants should not be older than 40 years of age

The Neurology Specialty Program is a three-year Clinical Residency Regular Program which trains general practitioners (GPs) in the field of Neurology. The Neurology program includes the study of General Neurology, Neurological History taking and physical Examination, Neuroanatomy and Neurolocalization, Neuroscience and Neuropathology, Neurological Critical care, Neurological Emergencies, Neurophysiology and basics of Psychiatry, Neuropediatric, Neurosurgery and Neuroradiology. At the end of the training, graduates will become full-fledged Neurologist in the following core competencies of the program: Patient Care, Medical Knowledge, Practice-Based Learning and Improvement, Systems-Based Practice, Professionalism, and Interpersonal and Communication Skills. Graduates will be able to run outpatient Neurology clinics, manage hospitalized patients with Neurologic problems, perform relevant neurophysiologic procedures independently, conduct relevant researches, rationally utilize available resources, and participate in health promotion and preventive activities concerning Neurologic problems. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the national residency matching exam set by the Ministry of Health of a general Practitioner with a Medical Degree from a recognized institution, as well as the Graduate Admission Test of Addis Ababa University. The candidate is required to pass the Entrance examination, prepared by the Department of Neurology as below. He/she should score >=70%. Candidates will also need to submit updated Curriculum Vitae, two recommendation letters including evidence for community service, personal motivational letter, and should be in good state of health both mentally and physically, that enables him/her to practice Neurology. Admission of foreign applicants shall be governed by the AAU Senate Legislation provided that the applicants fulfilled the other admission requirements.

The psychiatry residency program has led to a nine-fold expansion of psychiatrists from 12 in 2003 to more than 90 in 2024 Graduates from the psychiatry program at AAU have established regional psychiatry units in Jimma, Mekele, Adama, Awasa, Dire Dawa Bahir Dar, and Amanuel Specialized Mental Hospital. Three institutions headed by graduates of our training program have succeeded in establishing their own residency programs in psychiatry. While maintaining the high quality of the residency program, the department has now ongoing sub specialty programs and is also in the process of developing a number of subspecialty/fellowship training programs in Forensic psychiatry, Addiction Psychiatry, Psychotherapy, Old Age Psychiatry and Neuropsychiatry, Prenatal Psychiatry, Clinical Liaison, and Trauma Psychiatry.

Family & community Medicine is a 3 years and 6 months regular program, that studies medicine, emergency medicine [Including subspecialty clinic like Renal, Diabetes, GI,& Pulmonology as the residents inclination ], pediatrics [including pediatrics OPD & Pediatrics ICU], gynecology & obstetrics,[with hands on session performing CS] surgery, psychiatry, orthopedics, ophthalmology, Dermatology, radiology [POCUS], Palliative care, and geriatric medicine currently we are providing service in Macedonia we provide health care service to patients, many of patients are age old people. We are also providing teaching session to health workers in Macedonia on selected topics so that they have overview of geriatric care. The same is true to health center workers they will select topics and our residents and faculty provide teaching session. These are included in the teaching & learning process. Public health [writing thesis & defense at the end of the course] quality improvement & they will have to select gaps in the process of their training and prepare quality improvement project with the owners of the institute where they have identified gap, and present to the whole member of the staff so that the activities continue in improvement & continue the cycle. All the teaching process is having hands on practical sessions, patient encounter, morning presentation & discussions, bed side rounds. Academic Half Day once a week from 1:30pm 5:00pm on two selected topics which will be presented by 2 residents Staff faculty Ethiopian or When [TAAAC] Toronto Addis Ababa Academic Collaboration,] members are in Addis they will also be involved, we have regular annual program with them. We have also community engagements with AAU Catchment health centers where we are providing clinical services 2 days a week regularly & teaching session. And A one-month block attachments at year three. Admission criteria for the candidate, to join family medicine she/he, should be a graduate of a known medical school with her/his medical degree [medical doctor] and pass an entrance examination. [resident matching exam] It is also better for residents have clinical exposure, to coup with. As the residents are admitted to AAU department of Family and Community Medicine department we provide them a one-month solid preliminary training on main topics which they are encounter during the training. So that they are aware how the teaching and learning process is going and what is it includes and what is expected from them. Every resident will have a mentor [faculty member] he/she will have regular meeting. The meeting usually is called by the mentor but residents can also call to discuss maters they feel it is important for them & their concerns. We have a type of advisory committee [Academic progression committee (APC)] When a resident act/ behaving the wrong way from the AAU regulation, teaching learning process, any of the faculty member inform to his/her mentor, the resident will be called by his mentor sit for a discussion that is will be given advise/ warning not to do so further. If the resident repeated the same. He/she will be called by APC after open discussion will be given written warning. This is what it looks like life in Family Medicine in brief.

Dermatology and venereology specialty training is a 3 (three) years regular residency program. Dermatology is a medical specialty which deals with the diagnosis and treatment of children and adults with skin, Nail and Hair Diseases whereas. Venereology is an area that deals with the diagnosis, treatment and prevention of sexually transmitted infections (STI) along with promotion of good sexual health. A certified dermatologist and venereologist is expected to be an expert in disorders of the skin, nails, hair, and visible mucous membranes and STIs .The candidate's knowledge must include the basic science of the skin and its appendages, pharmacology, and therapeutics. It should also include photomedicine, dermatopathology, cutaneous diagnostic skills, venereology, the performance of cutaneous surgical procedures, the safe utilization of phototherapy and lasers, the basics of cosmetic dermatology, ethics, professionalism, and evidence-based medicine and research. Admission requirement for the specialty program is based on selection criteria set by the University and college of Health Sciences Medical graduates will undergo competitive selection into Dermatovenereology specialty training • The candidate must have successfully completed his/her basic medical training and graduated with an MD degree or its equivalent with successful completion of an internship • Candidates graduated from other countries must approve their degree at FMHACA that their degree is equivalent to the Ethiopian MD degree. • Have passed the selection exam and interview (if exists) • The candidate should be in acceptable mental and physical state

Internal Medicine is a branch of medicine concerned with the comprehensive care of adults. It encompasses a broad range of medical conditions and diseases that affect internal organs and systems. This Residency Training Program is a specialty program given to medical doctors for three years with the goal of producing the best possible physicians who practice on internal medicine using the best knowledge and standard scientific skills to meet the health care needs of society. During the three-year training, the internal medicine resident will be able to manage all aspects of infectious diseases, cardiac illnesses, hematologic diseases, pulmonary and critical illnesses, kidney diseases, neurologic diseases, gastrointestinal diseases, endocrine diseases, rheumatologic, and emergency conditions. The overall program requirements will be in line with to Ethiopian Ministry of Education, Ministry of Health and Addis Ababa university requirements. In order to join the program, the candidates must fulfill the following: • The candidate should be in a good state of health, both mentally and physically, that enables him/her to practice internal medicine. • Candidates shall register & sit for the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) • Eligible candidates should have an M.D degree from a recognized training program. • The candidate should include two letters of reference from supervisors or professional colleagues.

The Doctor of Medicine is a six and half year regular program that studies about the normal and abnormal body function of the human body, how to diagnose and manage a disease and how a disease can be prevented. It includes various healthcare practices aimed at restoring and maintaining health, such as pharmaceuticals, psychotherapy, and surgery. The program involves premed, preclinical, clinical and internship studies. In the premed (one-year studies) common courses including English language communication skills, history, geography, general chemistry and other courses are covered. Preclinical studies (one-year) lay the ground work for clinical medicine by providing an in-depth understanding of the scientific basis of health and disease, while also developing essential clinical and professional skills. The curriculum is designed to integrate basic and clinical sciences to prepare students for success in their clinical training. Progress from the preclinical to clinical year of study will be determined by internal evaluation of each module and Pre-clinical Qualifying Examination (Exit exam). The clinical year (two- and half-year) is a critical phase where students transition from more of theoretical knowledge to practical application in patient care. It emphasizes hands-on experience, the development of clinical skills, interprofessional collaboration, and critical thinking, all of which are essential for becoming competent and compassionate physicians. Before getting into internship students will have a Clinical Qualifying Examination. The internship year focuses on applying classroom the both pre-clinical and clinical year knowledge to real-world clinical situations, developing essential skills, and transitioning to independent practice as a physician. In the program students are regularly assessed through practical exams, written tests, oral exam, and evaluations by supervisors during clinical rotations.

The MSc in Clinical Trials program, the first of its kind in Africa and accredited internationally by a European accreditation agency, is designed to produce highly skilled professionals who will advance clinical trials research, services, and academic programs in Africa. The program aims to provide students with a deep understanding and skills of key decisions in designing, executing, and interpreting clinical trials while adhering to regulatory and legal frameworks. Graduates are expected to lead, monitor and regulate clinical trials in pharmaceutical industries, regulatory bodies, contract research organizations, research institutes, and academia. The program features modular coursework, seminars, industry attachments and internships, and an MSc thesis, all completed over one and half to two years. The ideal applicant should have a Bachelor’s degree in healthcare or life sciences, such as pharmacy, public health, nursing, biology, Doctor of Medicine (MD), Doctor of Dental Medicine (DDM), or Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM). Candidates with a Bachelor’s in Statistics are also eligible. Additional qualifications, with or without a Bachelor’s degree in life sciences, may be considered if relevant, such as a MSc in Public Health, Pharmacy, Pharmacology, Statistics, or a Doctor of Pharmacy. Ideally, as this is a need-based program, candidates should have one to two years of relevant experience post-graduation and should join the program with prior exposure to address specific challenges they have encountered

CHS.department_name: MSc in Clinical Laboratory Sciences (Clinical Chemistry)
CHS.courses: MSc in clinical laboratory science, clinical chemistry specialty is a two-year regular program that studies the advanced principles of laboratory science with clinical chemistry specialty such as advanced clinical chemistry, advanced endocrinology and tumor markers, clinical toxicology, instrumentation and quality assurance in clinical chemistry. The students start their journey by taking general and supportive courses such as pedagogy, biostatistics and research methodology, laboratory management and quality assurance, advanced immunology, microbial genetics, bioinformatics and molecular techniques. The program comes with a clinical practicum attachment in hospitals and advanced research laboratories to showcase the clinical chemistry courses’ application in basic and advanced laboratories. The students are also expected to submit a graduating thesis with the help of well-accomplished advisors. This program aims to produce clinical laboratory professionals capable of providing diagnostic laboratory services with clinical chemistry specialty, giving lectures on the subjects they have taken to undergraduate medical laboratory science students at higher learning institutions, serve as consultants/supervisors in improving and managing the quality of regional and national reference laboratories in addition to laboratories in health centers and hospitals, support laboratory accreditation as per ISO standards, write research proposals, conduct research project independently and prepare manuscripts for publication. Furthermore, the graduates are capable of advising on supply chain management of laboratory supplies and logistic systems, managing laboratory workforce, marketing, budgeting, healthcare management, devising appropriate laboratory quality management systems, developing laboratory policies, rules, and regulations, conducting risk management in diagnostic and research laboratories, consulting with regional and federal health ministries, facility design and equipment management, and various other responsibilities related to laboratory practices and research.

CHS.department_name: MSc in Laboratory Management & Quality Assurance
CHS.courses: MSc in Clinical Laboratory Science, Laboratory Management& Quality Assurance Specialty is a two year program that offers a multitude of courses such as advanced quality assurance, health laboratory information systems, advanced clinical laboratory management, health economics and managing and marketing laboratory medicine services which are intended to equip students with the knowledge and skills of enhancing and maintaining the quality of basic and advanced laboratories. The students start their journey by taking general and supportive courses such as pedagogy, biostatistics and research methodology, laboratory management and quality assurance, advanced immunology, microbial genetics, bioinformatics and molecular techniques. The students are also expected to submit a graduating thesis with the help of well-accomplished advisors. The program’s objective is to produce graduates capable of providing lectures on their expertise to undergraduate medical laboratory or other health sciences students at higher learning institutions. Furthermore, they are able to improve and manage the quality of regional and national reference laboratories plus laboratories in health centers and hospitals, support laboratory accreditation as per ISO standards, and contribute to the national laboratory diagnostic network and integrated quality disease surveillance (IDSR). In addition, graduates will be able to perform method validation and verification for newly established laboratory methods, advising on supply chain management of laboratory supplies and logistic systems, managing laboratory workforce, marketing, budgeting, healthcare management, devising appropriate laboratory quality management systems, developing laboratory policies, rules, and regulations, conducting risk management in diagnostic and research laboratories, consulting with regional and federal health ministries, facility design and equipment management, and various other responsibilities related to laboratory practices and research. Admission requirement for the program is based on having undergraduate degree in health sciences (provided with course audit), results of the NGAT (National graduate Admission Test), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE.

CHS.department_name: MSc in Clinical Laboratory Sciences (Diagnostic & Public Health Microbiology)
CHS.courses: MSc in Clinical Laboratory Science, Diagnostic &Public Health Microbiology Specialty is a two year program that will equip students with the knowledge and skills of clinical laboratory science specialized in diagnostic and public health microbiology with courses such as advanced public health microbiology, advanced diagnostic bacteriology, advanced diagnostic mycology, advanced diagnostic virology and advanced diagnostic parasitology. The students start their journey by taking general and supportive courses such as pedagogy, biostatistics and research methodology, laboratory management and quality assurance, advanced immunology, microbial genetics, bioinformatics and molecular techniques. The program is complete with clinical practicum attachment at different hospitals and advanced research laboratories to sharpen the students’ practical skills and enhance their educational experience. The students are expected to submit a graduating thesis with the help of well-accomplished advisors. This program aims to produce clinical laboratory professionals capable of providing diagnostic laboratory services especially public health microbiology, giving lectures on the subjects they have taken to undergraduate medical laboratory students and other health sciences students in higher learning institutions, serve as consultants/supervisors in improving as well as managing the quality of regional and national reference laboratories in addition to laboratories in health centers and hospitals, support laboratory accreditation as per ISO standards, contribute to the national laboratory diagnostic network and integrated quality disease surveillance (IDSR), write research proposals, conduct research project independently and prepare manuscripts for publication. Furthermore, graduates will be capable of advising on supply chain management of laboratory supplies and logistic systems, managing laboratory workforce, marketing, budgeting, healthcare management, devising appropriate laboratory quality management systems, developing laboratory policies, rules, and regulations, conducting risk management in diagnostic and research laboratories, consulting with regional and federal health ministries, facility design and equipment management, and various other responsibilities related to laboratory practices and research

CHS.department_name: MSc in Clinical Laboratory Sciences (Hematology & Immunohematology
CHS.courses: MSc in clinical laboratory science, hematology and immunohematology specialty, is a two year program that equips students with the knowledge and skills of advanced hematology, advanced hematology techniques, advanced immunohematology, blood transfusion and transplant science and histotechnology. The students start their journey by taking general and supportive courses such as pedagogy, biostatistics and research methodology, laboratory management and quality assurance, advanced immunology, microbial genetics, bioinformatics and molecular techniques. The program is complete with clinical practicum attachment where students are assigned to hospitals, advanced laboratories and the National Blood & Tissue bank to sharpen their skills as well as enhance their educational experiences. Students are expected to complete their research project with the help of well-seasoned advisors. This program aims to produce clinical laboratory professionals capable of providing diagnostic laboratory services with hematology & immunohematology specialty, giving lectures on the subjects they have taken to undergraduate medical laboratory science or other health science students at higher learning institutions, serve as consultants/supervisors on improving quality of regional and national reference labs in addition to laboratories in health centers and hospitals, support laboratory accreditation as per ISO standards, write research proposals, conduct research project independently and prepare a manuscript for publication. Furthermore, the graduates are capable of advising on supply chain management of laboratory supplies and logistic systems, managing laboratory workforce, marketing, budgeting, healthcare management, devising appropriate laboratory quality management systems, developing laboratory policies, rules, and regulations, conducting risk management in diagnostic and research laboratories, consulting with regional and federal health ministries, facility design and equipment management, and various other responsibilities related to laboratory practices and research.

The MSc in Medical Microbiology is a two-year track-based graduate program designed to provide an in-depth understanding of medically important microorganisms, such as bacteria, viruses, fungi, and parasites; their pathogenesis; immune responses; and the methodologies used to diagnose and treat infectious diseases. This program equips students with the theoretical knowledge and practical skills needed to work in clinical diagnostics, research, public health, and related fields. Students will receive a firm foundation in basic and public health courses before they proceed into their specific tracks that include Medical Microbiology, Medical Parasitology, and Medical Immunology. During the two years of MSc training, students will receive both in-depth theoretical knowledge and hands-on training in different teaching, clinical, and research laboratories; and undertake original research under the supervision of a faculty advisor. Admission requirements for the program are based on the applicant's previous academic record, the choice of the student, and the intake capacity of the department as well as GAT (Graduate Admission Test) offered by the University. The applicant must meet all the admission requirements of graduate programs and all the criteria set by the admission policy of Addis Ababa University. The applicant should hold a BSc degree in Medical Laboratory Sciences, Doctor of Medicine, Doctor of Veterinary Medicine, BSc in Applied Biology, Bachelor of Pharmacy, BSc in Public Health Officer, or BSc in Nursing and related fields from a recognized academic institution. Foreign examination results/academic credentials must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency.

The master’s program in the Department is also the first and leading in its establishment and producing many research-oriented professionals nationally. We aim to produce research-oriented physiologists who support the shortage of teaching staff in medical schools and researchers in research institutions. It is a two-year program focused on basic and advanced courses accompanied by research works. The program exposes students to studies involving many human organ functions and do research in biomedical sciences which can enhance and improve the clinical practices in teaching hospitals found in the country and at large globally. The advanced courses and the research are important for them to develop better future careers in education, research, and community services. The admission requirement for the program is based on the obligations an applicant should fulfill like in other universities in the country and AAU senate legislation. Specifically, a minimum CGPA of 2:00 during the undergraduate programs mentioned below, passing the Graduation Admission Test (GAT) of the AAU, and both written and oral exams given by the Department are other admission requirements. A candidate who holds a degree in Medicine (MD) or B.Sc. in Pharmacy, Biology, Health Officer, Nursing, midwifery, Medical Laboratory Technology, Dentistry, Anesthesia, and Sport Science from government universities and other recognized institutions of higher learning can be admitted to the program provided that they fulfill the above requirements.

The M.Sc. in Medical Biochemistry is a comprehensive two-year graduate program designed to cultivate highly qualified and proficient biochemists who possess professional expertise in teaching, research and consultancy. This program aims to address the critical shortage of skilled professionals in the field within universities and across the country. Students will develop a robust foundational knowledge essential for effective teaching, conducting research, and providing consultancy services in biochemistry. They will also gain insights into designing and implementing research projects pertinent to biochemistry, cell biology and molecular biology. Furthermore, the curriculum emphasizes advocating for modern principles and applications of contemporary techniques utilized in biochemistry and molecular biology. The admission requirements for the program stipulate that applicants must be graduates holding degrees such as MD, DVM, BPharm, BScRT, DDM, BSc MLT, or BScN from this or other universities, with a minimum grade of “B” in Biochemistry or a cumulative GPA of 2.50 upon graduation. Additionally, candidates with a Bachelor of Science in Biochemistry, Biomedical Sciences, Molecular Biology, Chemistry, Biology, or Zoology must possess an upper second class degree and also meet the same minimum grade requirement in Biochemistry or cumulative GPA. It is mandatory for all graduates to have completed at least a 3-credit hour course in Biochemistry. Furthermore, the general admission policies and criteria established by the University are applicable. Applicants are required to submit their current academic credentials including an official transcript, certified degree documentation, and three letters of recommendation from previous instructors and employers. Lastly, candidates must successfully pass both written and oral entrance examinations administered by the department.

The MSc in Medical Anatomy is a two-year regular program that focusses on gross anatomy, microscopic anatomy and developmental anatomy in relation to their clinical correlations. Students studying MSc in medical anatomy able to teach gross anatomy, histology and embryology courses to medical and paramedical students as well as residents. In this MSc program the students will be equipped with knowledge and skill of teaching, dissection, performing different histological techniques, and doing research in different disciplines of biomedical sciences as well as providing community service to the public. Admission requirement for the program is based on the admission criteria set by the university senate legislation for graduate programs, credentials of the candidates and also passing GAT (Graduate Admission Test). Candidates must have any one of the following degrees: MD (Medicine), DDM (Doctor of Dental Medicine), BDS (Bachelor of Dental Science), DVM/B.V.M (Veterinary Medicine), Bachelor degree in Anatomy, Public Health, Biology, Pharmacy, Nursing, Midwifery, Anesthesia, Physiotherapy, Laboratory, and Radiography. Finally the students should also pass the written and/or oral exams given by the Department of Anatomy. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE

The M.Sc. in Neonatal Nursing builds upon the baccalaureate foundation and prepares graduates for advanced neonatal nursing care. The program will be delivered in two-year fulltime learning mode. In order to meet the learning outcome, the learning process will be fully clinical (residential approach) except the first semester (four month) which is a theoretical common course and will be given in the classroom. Admission requirement for the program is based on the general regulations of the Addis Ababa University. Applicants must have completed a baccalaureate degree in Nursing with a minimum of one year of clinical/community experience after a B.Sc. nursing. Two letters of recommendations from professionals who can attest to the applicant’s ability. Should pass the entrance examination set by the Department.

The Master of Science in Advanced Nursing Practice is a two-year regular program designed to train nurses for advanced practice in specialized fields. The program offers three specialty tracks: Oncology Nursing, Cardiovascular Nursing, and Perioperative Cardiothoracic Nursing. It emphasizes evidence-based practice, leadership, and research, preparing graduates to meet complex healthcare challenges and improve patient outcomes. The program consists of core courses, specialty-specific courses, clinical internships, and a thesis. Delivered through a residency approach, the first five months focus on coursework, while the remaining 16 months are dedicated to clinical practice and research. Admission to the program is based on passing the postgraduate admission tests (PGAT) and holding a bachelor's degree in nursing from recognized school of nursing; two letters of recommendation from professionals who can attest to the applicant’s ability; and passing the department's entrance exam.

The master of Sciences in Emergency Nursing is a two-year program offering two tracks: Emergency and Critical Care Nurse Specialist, and Emergency and Trauma Nurse Practitioner. This program empowers students with advanced skills in trauma, emergency, and critical care nursing, enhancing critical thinking, leadership, and collaboration. It promotes lifelong learning and professional development. Students will engage in innovative research aimed at improving emergency nursing practices and addressing Ethiopia's healthcare challenges, with findings shared to enhance clinical practice. Graduates of this program will deliver exceptional nursing care, improve community health outcomes, support ongoing education for nurses, collaborate with health authorities to strengthen the workforce, and engage in outreach to highlight the significance of trauma and critical care nursing. Admission Requirements: Admission to the program is based on the student's preferences, departmental capacity, and the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by the University. Specific departmental requirements include: A Bachelor's degree in clinical nursing, critical care nursing , emergency nursing, or health officer or anesthesia with nursing background from an accredited institution. A minimum of one years of clinical experience within the past four years for applicants from clinical backgrounds and teaching experience for academic area applicants. Successful completion of the written and oral entrance examination administered by the hosting department.

The M.Sc. in Nursing is a two-year regular program that prepare qualified professional nurses for specialized nursing practice roles. The program will expose students to evidence-based learning teaching environments to prepare the graduates with specialized nursing skills. The program will offer three specialty tracks: Adult health nursing, Maternity Health Nursing, and Child Health Nursing. It enables graduates to assume responsibilities as nurse specialists, consultants, educators, administrators, and researchers in specialized professional settings.

CHS.department_name: MPH in Health Promotion and Health Education
CHS.courses: The Master of Public Health (MPH) in Health Promotion and Health Education is a two-year regular program designed to cultivate innovative public health leaders equipped with the skills to improve community health and well-being. The program integrates theoretical knowledge and practical experience, beginning with foundational public health concepts such as epidemiology and health behavior, and advancing to specialized areas like health communication, program planning, and policy analysis. Students gain practical experience through internships and a research thesis, preparing them for leadership roles in various public health settings. Admission to the program is based on holding a first degree in one of the following fields: Public Health, Medicine, Dental Medicine, Radiology, Nursing, Environmental Health, Midwifery, Medical Laboratory Science, Optometry, Anesthesia, Health Education, Pharmacy, Biology, Sociology, Journalism, Psychology, or Anthropology, and passing the National Graduate Admission Test (NGAT). Applicants may also need to pass an entrance examination and interview specific to the program. Foreign examination results must be certified for equivalency to national standards.

CHS.department_name: MPH in Environmental and Occupational Health
CHS.courses: The Master of Public Health (MPH) in Environmental and Occupational Health is a two-year program designed to equip students with a comprehensive understanding of how environmental factors and workplace conditions influence health and well-being. Students specializing in this field will gain in-depth theoretical knowledge and practical skills necessary for designing, implementing, and evaluating interventions in environmental and occupational health. They will also develop strong competencies in conducting health research, enabling them to contribute to solutions for major health challenges in these areas. During the thesis research period, students are expected to undertake rigorous research projects that address significant issues in environmental and occupational health, thereby contributing to the advancement of public health knowledge and practice. For admission to the program, the candidate must have a bachelor's degree in one of the following fields: Environmental Health, Occupational Health and Safety, Public Health, Medicine, Environmental Sciences, Environmental Technology, Chemistry, Biology, and any health-related fields. All candidates must pass the National Graduate Admission Test (NGAT) and pass an examination administered by the school for program admission. Foreign examination results must be certified for equivalency to national standards

The graduate program in Master of Public Health with a focus on Health Economics is meticulously crafted to endow students with vital competencies and insights needed to thrive in the intricate domain of health economics. The curriculum delves into economic evaluation, data analytics, and the economic frameworks within the healthcare sector. It harmonizes intensive academic learning with hands-on experience, enhancing the students' capabilities in evaluating and enhancing healthcare's worth. An integral part of the educational journey is the capstone project, which empowers students to deploy their learned skills on tangible health policy issues. Graduates of this program are esteemed for students’ substantial role in the judicious and fair distribution of healthcare resources, leveraging evidence from research to shape policymaking. Prospective students aiming to join the MPH in Health Economics must possess an undergraduate degree (BSc/BA/MD/DVM) from a recognized institution.

CHS.department_name: MPH in Reproductive, Family and Population Health
MPH in Health Systems Management and Health Policy
CHS.courses: Masters of Public Health in Health Systems Management and Health Policy is a two-year graduate program in the school of Public Health, College of Health Sciences, Addis Ababa University. The main aim of the program is to provide public health professionals with a basic foundation of knowledge and skill in public health, health policy and health system management focusing at producing higher caliber academic and service-oriented graduates that can serve universities, research institutes, and government and non-governmental organizations including international organizations, and engaged in policy matters. An integral part of the educational journey is the capstone project, which empowers students to solve tangible health problems and policy issues by applying knowledge gained through the program which leads to the development of their technical skills, cognitive abilities, and interpersonal competencies. The program is also designed to help students gain global health orientation so that they can contribute to the advancement of global health. For admission into MPH in Health Systems Management and Health Policy program the prospective candidates must • Have first degree (BSc/BA/MD/DVM) from an accredited higher institution; • Must pass Addis Ababa University’s Graduate Admission Test (GAT); • In addition, all candidates should fulfill the admission requirements of the School of Graduate Studies of the University. Specifically, the candidate Should have educational background in health and health related professions; health related professions include, but not limited to, the following: o Management, Economics and Sociology

The Masters in Public Health Nutrition is a two-year regular program that is intended to produce qualified public health Nutrition professionals who are able to identify the relationships among nutrients and food intake patterns, plan, design, implement and evaluate nutrition intervention programs , have a firm foundation in the principles of population and community-based nutrition interventions and evaluations, acquire epidemiologic tools on nutrition and health; and the relationships among population consumption patterns, nutrition and health, acquire practical skills in planning, implementing and reporting health research using both qualitative and quantitative research methods, have firm foundations in policy processes (domestic and international), typologies of policy initiatives (laws, global initiatives, interventions, legal restrictions, systems, and institutional mandates), and critically analyze and discuss how policy and science interact with regard to food and nutrition. Admission requirement for the program requires that a candidate should have a first degree from a known and accredited university in nutrition, public health officer, medical doctor and other health related fields. All candidates should fulfill the admission requirement of the School of Graduate Studies of Addis Ababa University (AAU). Foreign applicants should bring English language proficiency certificate from their previous University or should bring TOEFL/IELTS exam scores. Candidates must obtain a pass mark in Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by AAU and department specific entrance examination.Applicants who fulfill the admission requirement will be invited to sit for an entrance written and/or oral examination that will mostly have general knowledge questions related to nutrition, biostatistics, epidemiology, reproductive health, health system management, environmental health and behavioral health science. Sitting for an interview will depend on the result of the written examination. The interview will mainly focus on general knowledge on nutrition, research experience and communication skills.

The Master of Science Program in Health Informatics is offered in a regular and extension program. The regular modality is a two-year program, while the extension program is a three-year. The program involves course work, capstone project, internship and Thesis. The courses focus on the efficient health data collection, management, use of health information and developing a software that able to captures health information. Students studying this program will start from common courses and basic Health Informatics courses. Once students are familiar with basic health informatics courses, they will be introduced gradually to the advanced health informatics courses. The capstone project gives them an opportunity where they plan and implement a project, while internship will help them exposed to the practice of health informatics activities in the facilities or health offices. The program is hosted by School of Public Health and run jointly with the School of Information Science.

The MPH in Field Epidemiology is part of the global brand the Field Epidemiology and Laboratory Training Program (FELTP) with an international accreditation from TEPHINET. This is a two-year competency-based field residency training and service program in applied epidemiology and public health that builds the capacity to strengthen the surveillance and public health response systems in Ethiopian and other countries. The training is primarily based on field residency training where residents spend 75% of their time. Course modules are arranged into two blocks, which prepare the residents for two block of extensive field residencies to develop hands on skills and be engaged with institutional activities through epidemiologic in surveillance systems, outbreak investigation, epidemiologic research, and systems evaluation techniques. All the residents will produce a body of work and a final field research (thesis) as partial fulfilment for their graduation

The MPH in Epidemiology and Biostatistics is a two-years regular program that trains students to design impactful population health studies and translate research into tangible health improvements. Students will gain an in-depth understanding of the distribution, determinants, and prevention of disease, epidemiological study designs, methods and operations, infectious and chronic disease epidemiology, policy aspects, data analytics, data interpretation, and implementation and evaluation of health-related interventions and programs. This program is fundamental to public health and serves as a principal measuring instrument of health utilized to perform public health assessment functions. Graduates of this program will be equipped to confidently design, conduct, and analyze clinical, population-based, environmental, policy, and methodological health-related research and programs. The Master of Public Health (with Specialty in Health Research Ethics Track) is a two-year postgraduate masters training concentrating on health research ethics, which is offered through the Addis Ababa University (AAU) School of Public Health (SPH) in collaboration with Johns Hopkins University (JHU) in the US. Qualifying candidates will register for an active participation in course modules including practicum (fieldwork) visits and attachments at selected agencies, organizations and committees relevant to their interests in research ethics. In the first year, the program provides students with modules on the basic tools and principles of public health in the first semester, followed in the second semester by highly specialized modules specific to foundation of ethics in general and research ethics in particular augmented by practical sessions and case studies. The second year is dedicated for mentored thesis project where trainees are expected to conduct health research to address significant issues of the area of research ethics. Admission requirements for the program include first degree in Medicine (MD or DVM), a bachelor's degree in public health, nursing, environmental health, and statistics; as well as Bachelor’s degrees in biomedical studies and Natural Sciences, Social sciences and Humanities. All general requirements for admission to all Master’s Degrees in the University set forth for all schools of graduate studies shall apply to the program. As part of the AAU admission requirements candidates must obtain a pass mark in the National Graduate Admission Tests (NGAT) exam. Applicants will also be required to submit an admission application form, official transcripts of their academic records from their undergraduate studies, a statement of objectives/motivation, a resume/biodata, and two letters of recommendation.
