Furthermore, successful completion of an admission examination and interview is necessary to evaluate each candidate's academic preparation for pursuing a graduate specialty track and undertaking future assignments.

CHS.department_name: MSc in Pharmaceutical Analysis and Quality Assurance
CHS.courses: We are pleased to announce the launch of our Master of Science (MSc) in Pharmaceutical Analysis and Quality Assurance program, a two-year course designed to equip students with the essential skills and knowledge needed for careers in the pharmaceutical industry. This program covers advanced topics such as analytical techniques, quality control, and regulatory affairs, combining theoretical knowledge with practical laboratory experience. Graduates will be prepared for diverse roles in quality assurance, regulatory compliance, and research and development. Applications are now open for aspiring professionals looking to enhance their expertise and contribute to the evolving field of pharmaceuticals. Admission requirements include a bachelor’s degree in Pharmacy, Chemistry, or a related field, transcripts from previous academic institutions, letters of recommendation, and a personal statement outlining career goals (optional). Interested candidates are encouraged to apply early. For more information please contact: Email: chs.pharmchemcog@aau.edu.et Phone: +251118697104 Telegram: AAU, SoP, Pharmaceutical Chemistry & Pharmacognosy

CTBE.department_name: BSc in Construction Technology Management
CTBE.courses: BSc in Construction Technology and Management BSc in Construction Technology and Management is a five-year regular program that studies the basic principles of science and application of construction management, project Management in construction, Resource planning and Optimization in construction, Computer based Planning and schedule in construction, Construction law, procurement and contract management in construction and other related courses which is useful to lay a foundation for architecture, civil engineering and other social and business courses. Construction Technology and Management (COTM) is crucial for the development of modern society, integrating science with practical applications to drive both social and economic progress. This field encompasses a broad understanding of scientific and engineering principles, materials science, construction techniques, and the ability to analyze and solve complex problems. It also requires skills in research, teamwork, leadership, and business management. In Ethiopia, Construction Technology and Management holds significant potential for supporting economic growth and improving living standards. As the country experiences rapid development, Construction Technology and Management can play a key role in ensuring that this growth is sustainable. The sector's impact spans various scales, from federal to local levels, and includes both urban and rural contexts. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE

CTBE.department_name: BSc in Urban and Regional Planning
BSc in Architecture and Design
CTBE.courses: BSc in Architecture BSc in Architecture is a five year regular program that studies about designing suitable built structures for human habitat. Since Architecture is one of the key professions involved in shaping the built environment and urban spaces, the education equips students with necessary skills and knowledge to be competitive, creative, critically minded and ethical professional designers/builders who are able to design built structures often involving potential contradictions between different requirements. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination [EHEECE], the choice of student, the capacity of Department/School and the UGAT [undergraduate Admission Test] offered by the University as well as the screening exam offered by the Department/School. Foreign examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE.

CTBE.department_name: Bachelor of Science in Software Engineering and Computing Technology (Cyber security, artificial intelligence, Software Engineering, Information technology}
CTBE.courses: The BSc. In Software Engineering and Computing Technology program is a five-years undergraduate program designed to provide students with a comprehensive understanding of the principles, methodologies, and tools required to design, develop, and maintain complex software systems and advanced computing solutions. The program integrates four key streams: Software Engineering, Information Technology, Artificial Intelligence, and Cybersecurity, offering a multidisciplinary approach that prepares graduates to address the challenges of a rapidly evolving digital landscape. Students will gain hands-on experience in software development, systems administration, data management, AI-driven solutions, and secure computing practices, equipping them with the skills and knowledge necessary to innovate and lead in diverse technology-driven industries. Admission requirement for program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of the School as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE.

CTBE.department_name: Bachelor of Science in Biomedical Engineering
CTBE.courses: The BSc in Biomedical Engineering is a five-year regular program, including the freshman program, or a six-year evening program that focuses on application of engineering principles and technological advances to solve problems in medicine and life sciences and thereby modernize healthcare services. Biomedical Engineering is a broad field of study which encompasses a range of concentration areas including, but not limited to, bio-instrumentation, medical imaging, biomechanics and rehabilitation, bio-materials and tissue engineering, bio-computing, and regenerative medicine. The program at AAU focuses on bio-instrumentation, medical imaging, bio computing, and biomechanics and rehabilitation. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE.

CTBE.department_name: Bachelor of Science in Electrical and Computer Engineering
CTBE.courses: Electrical and Computer Engineering (ECE) is a dynamic field that combines the principles of electrical engineering with computer engineering. ECE engineers design, develop, and implement technologies that drive our modern world. They work with electrical circuits, electronic devices, software, and hardware to create innovative solutions across various industries. ECE engineers play a crucial role in advancing technology in fields like healthcare, transportation, energy, and entertainment. They are responsible for innovations such as smartphones, renewable energy systems, medical devices, and autonomous vehicles. At fourth year of their education students will choose and specialize in either of the following key specialization areas in Electrical and Computer Engineering. Computer Engineering: Creating hardware and software for computers, servers, and networking systems. It also includes analyzing and manipulating signals, such as audio, video, and data, to extract information or improve quality. Communication Engineering: Developing technologies for transmitting and receiving information, including wireless networks and telecommunications systems. Control Engineering: Designing and implementing systems that regulate and control the behavior of other systems, such as robots or automated processes. Electrical Power Engineering: Developing and managing electrical power generation, transmission, and distribution systems. Electronics: Designing and developing electronic components and systems, such as microprocessors, integrated circuits, and sensors. Admission requirement for program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of the School as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE

CTBE.department_name: Bachelor of Science in Mechanical Engineering
CTBE.courses: Mechanical engineering is a versatile and broad field that focuses on the design, development, and manufacturing of a wide range of mechanical systems, devices, and products. Mechanical engineering UG programs provide a rigorous education in the fundamental principles of mathematics, physics, materials science, thermodynamics, fluid mechanics, mechanics, and design. Students in a mechanical engineering UG program learn to apply these principles to analyze, model, and design mechanical components and systems, such as engines, HVAC systems, robotics, aerospace vehicles, and medical devices. They develop skills in computer-aided design (CAD), prototyping, testing, and project management. The curriculum typically includes courses in areas like, Mathematics, mechanics, thermodynamics, heat transfer, dynamics, materials, manufacturing, and design. Students also gain hands-on experience through labs and projects. Many programs offer opportunities for undergraduate research and internships as well. Graduates of mechanical engineering UG programs are prepared for careers in industries like aerospace, automotive, energy, manufacturing, and healthcare, or to pursue further graduate study. They have a versatile skillset that allows them to contribute to a wide range of technological innovations and solutions. The applicant must pass mark on Secondary Education Certificate Examination and AAU Entrance exam (UGAT). Moreover, students who have successfully completed the 10 plus 4 years preparatory or completed 12th grade and have scored the minimum cut-off point set by the Ministry of Education in the entrance exam and diploma graduates could apply for admission to the Mechanical Engineering BSc program. In view of the high number of applicants, admission to the school is rather competitive. Entrance exam will be given to select competitive candidates. The maximum capacity of the school is 200 students.

CTBE.department_name: Bachelor of Science in Chemical Engineering
CTBE.courses: The BSc program in Chemical Engineering at the School of Chemical and Bio Engineering, AAiT, AAU aims to produce graduates who are prepared to tackle industrial, environmental, and social challenges. This program provides students with a strong foundation in a multidisciplinary field that incorporates chemistry, physics, biology, economics, and engineering principles. Students gain practical experience in chemical process design, equipment handling and analysis, and process control. The program emphasizes hands-on learning, fostering the ability to define, analyze, and solve engineering challenges using modern tools and techniques. Furthermore, it focuses on enabling students to design and optimize chemical processes while considering safety, sustainability, and economic factors. It also enhances communication skills, teamwork, ethical and professional responsibility, and promotes lifelong learning to adapt to technological advancements. These objectives ensure that graduates are well-prepared to contribute to the chemical engineering profession and tackle industrial and environmental challenges effectively. The admission requirements for the program are determined by the results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the student's choice of program, and the capacity of the Departments/Schools/Centers. Additionally, the University also offers the Undergraduate Admission Test (UGAT) for admission consideration. For applicants with foreign examination results, it is necessary to have them certified by the Ministry of Education to determine their equivalency to the EHEECE.

CTBE.department_name: Bachelor of Science in Civil Engineering
CTBE.courses: The BSc program in Civil Engineering is a five-years degree program and it concerns the study of conception, design, construction and maintenance of large public and private projects. Civil engineers build and maintain bridges, highways, railways, tunnels, airports, dams, water treatment and distribution systems and large buildings, along with many other structures. Environmental considerations, such as water supply, pollution control and preservation of soil quality, are also a part of the course of study. With thorough knowledge of both the principles of construction and the possible environmental consequences of a structure, the civil engineer's expertise is one that is essential. This program is aimed at training manpower required for the realization of the country’s untouched civil engineering works. The applicant must pass mark on Secondary Education Certificate Examination and AAU entrance exam (UGAT). Moreover, students who have successfully completed the 10 plus 2 years preparatory or completed 12th grade and have scored the minimum cut-off point set by the Ministry of Education in the entrance exam and diploma graduates could apply for admission to the Civil Engineering BSc program. In view of the high number of applicants, admission to the school is rather competitive. Entrance exam will be given to select competitive candidates

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Infrastructure Planning and Management
CTBE.courses: MSc in Infrastructure Planning and Management: The MSc in Infrastructure Planning and Management is a two-year full-time program that focuses on the interdisciplinary study of infrastructure systems, including water supply, wastewater management, transport, energy, and telecommunications. Students progress from foundational knowledge in infrastructure planning to advanced skills in integrating and managing complex infrastructure projects. The program equips students with the ability to analyze and design sustainable infrastructure solutions that address the needs of developing and newly industrialized countries. Throughout the program, students gain practical experience in infrastructure planning and management through a combination of coursework, fieldwork, and research. In their final semester, students undertake a thesis project that allows them to apply their knowledge to real-world challenges in infrastructure development. Admission requirements for the program are based on holding a Bachelor of Science degree in Civil Engineering, Construction Technology and Management, Architecture and Urban Planning, or related fields. Admission is determined by the results of entrance exams, the candidate's academic record, and the capacity of the program. Foreign qualifications must be certified by the relevant authorities to ensure they meet the standards of the Ethiopian Higher Education system.

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Urban Planning and Design
CTBE.courses: MSc in Urban Planning and Design The MSc in Urban Planning and Design program is offered by the Ethiopian Institute of Architecture, Building Construction, and City Development (EiABC) at Addis Ababa University. This two-year program provides specialized training through two distinct tracks: Urban Planning and Urban Design. The need for qualified individuals with experience in urban planning and design is more than ever as Ethiopia embarks on a massive urban development project. The program aims to provide students with the advanced abilities necessary to fulfill this increasing demand. It also equips students with the skills to independently work in urban planning and design, provide expert advice, and conduct research in these fields. For the MSc in Urban Planning and Design, particularly the Urban Planning specialization, applicants must hold a Bachelor of Science degree in Urban Planning and Design, Urban and Regional Planning, Urban Engineering, Architecture, Architecture and Urban Planning, or closely related social science fields such as Geography, Economics, Sociology, or other relevant disciplines that include coursework in urban and regional planning. Candidates for the Urban Design track is required to have completed a B.Sc. degree in Architecture, Landscape Architecture, Urban and Regional Planning, or a related field, and must also have at least two years of relevant work experience. Program Title: Doctor of Philosophy in Statisti

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Property Valuation and Management
MSc in Sustainable Housing and Environment
MSc in Integrated Architectural Design
MSc in Conservation of Urban and Architectural Heritage
CTBE.courses: Colorectal and Anal Surgery Sub-specialty Program Description SUB-SPECIALITY IN COLORECTAL AND ANAL SURGERY: The sub-specialty in Colorectal and Anal surgery is a two-year regular clinical training program that provides advanced training in the comprehensive and multidisciplinary management of diseases of the colon, rectum and anus. The Colorectal and anal Surgery fellow will be involved in every aspect of care as patients move through the healthcare system, including outpatient evaluation in two different settings; arranging for evaluation services such as laboratory and radiology services; decisions regarding least costly yet medically appropriate care fall patients; explanation of therapy alternatives to patients and families, making clear the risk-benefit ratio for the individual patient; working with fellow healthcare providers in other specialties to deliver high quality, safe patient care as a multidisciplinary team; and arranging for discharge when ready to an appropriate facility. Trainee selection will be the responsibility of the AAU-CHS, School of Medicine, department of surgery, colorectal surgery unit. Preference will be given to applicants who show excellent performances on their graduate program and a long term commitment to serve the human resource needs of Ethiopia and those who have practice and development support (sponsorship) in hand specialty from teaching medical institutions training surgeons in government institutions. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Addis Ababa University for their equivalency

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Landscape Architecture
MSc in Renewable Energy Technology Engineering
CTBE.courses: The MSc. in Renewable Energy Engineering at Addis Ababa University-Addis Ababa Institute of Technology (AAiT) is a two-year full-time program designed for graduate students to study the design, modelling and optimization, development, testing, implementation and operation of renewable energy technologies and systems. Students will engage in coursework, research, and practical projects, gaining hands-on experience in areas such as solar, wind, hydro, and bioenergy systems, integrating energy efficiency, sustainability and climate change mitigation through sustainable energy solutions. Students will have the opportunity to work on international research projects, such as Horizon Europe EU initiatives. They can collaborate with and benefit an academic guidance from our international partner institutions, fostering future partnerships and joint research with global experts in renewable energy. Admission to the program requires a bachelor's degree in engineering, science, or a related field, and passing score for the National Graduate Admission Test (NGAT) and entrance exam administered by the Center of Renewable Energy-AAiT. The program aims to produce skilled professionals to drive innovation, capable of addressing energy challenges and contributing to Ethiopia's sustainable energy transitions, preparing graduates for careers in industry, academia, and entrepreneurship.

CTBE.department_name: MSc/MEng in Artificial Intelligence
CTBE.courses: The MSc in Artificial Intelligence postgraduate program is designed to equip students with advanced knowledge and skills in the theory, development, and application of AI technologies. This program focuses on key areas such as machine learning, deep learning, natural language processing, computer vision, and robotics, providing a robust foundation for solving complex problems across various domains. Students will engage in hands-on projects, research, and practical applications to develop innovative AI solutions that address real-world challenges. Graduates will be prepared to take on leadership roles in AI research, development, and deployment across diverse sectors, including healthcare, finance, technology, and education. Applicants for the MSc in Artificial Intelligence program must hold a BSc degree with a minimum of 42 ECTS in core courses related to a computing discipline. Admission to the program also requires passing the school’s entrance exam as well as the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by the University. For applicants with foreign qualifications, examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education to confirm their equivalency to the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE).

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Cyber security (With Cybersecurity Governance and Management track, Network and Systems Security track, Cryptography, Cyber Intelligence and Warfare track, Digital Forensic and Cybercrime Investigation track)
CTBE.courses: The MSc in Cybersecurity program is designed to provide advanced skills and specialized knowledge in various domains of cybersecurity, including cryptography, cyber intelligence, network and systems security, digital forensics, and cybersecurity governance. This program aims to develop professionals who can tackle complex cybersecurity challenges, implement effective security measures, and contribute to the protection of digital assets in various organizational contexts. Graduates will be well-equipped to assume critical roles in both the public and private sectors, including positions such as security analysts, cybersecurity engineers, forensic investigators, and cyber intelligence officers. Applicants for the MSc in Cybersecurity program must hold a BSc degree in a computing discipline. Admission to the program also requires passing the school’s entrance exam as well as the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by the University. For applicants with foreign qualifications, examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education to confirm their equivalency to the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE)

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Railway Engineering (Traction and Train Control)
CTBE.courses: MSc in Railway Engineering (Traction and Train Control) is a two-year post graduate program that studies traction power system, train control, overhead contact line system, operations, and maintenance practices, design and analysis of traction power systems, design and analyze railway communication and control systems, railway system planning, operation and management. The students will equip with both theoretical and practical knowledge, and can design and analyze traction power system, communication system and controlling systems. Admission requirement for the program is based on the general application and admission policy of the Addis Ababa University and Ethiopian Ministry of Education. The applicant shall have a B.Sc. degree in Electrical Engineering or its equivalent and pass an entrance examination administered by African Railway Centre of Excellence (ARCE). Foreign applicants whose native language is other than English and the medium of instruction not English, a language test for proficiency is required.

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Railway Engineering (Rolling Stock Engineering)
CTBE.courses: MSc in Railway Engineering (Rolling stock) is a two-year post graduate program that studies Rolling stock design (braking system, Rail motive power, diesel traction power plants, wagon design, rail vehicle structural design, wheel-set design), Rolling stock operation and maintenance, Rolling stock incident investigation and vehicle performance monitoring, Rolling stock planning, operation and management. The students will equip with both theoretical and practical knowledge, and can design rolling stock main structure and its components, railway vehicle interaction with other system components, analyse the material properties and contact mechanics that affect wheel/rail performance, develop and apply mathematical models to analyse the dynamic behaviour of rail vehicles.   Admission requirement for the program is based on the general application and admission policy of the Addis Ababa University and Ethiopian Ministry of Education. The applicant shall have a B.Sc. degree in Mechanical Engineering or its equivalent and pass an entrance examination administered by African Railway Centre of Excellence (ARCE). Foreign applicants whose native language is other than English and the medium of instruction not English, a language test for proficiency is required.

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Railway Engineering (Civil Infrastructure)
CTBE.courses: MSc in Railway Engineering (Civil Infrastructure) is a two-year post graduate program that studies Railway alignment and track structure design, Railway infrastructure planning, operation and management, railway tunneling, railway bridge and associated civil infrastructure design, and train to track interaction. The students will equip with both theoretical and practical knowledge, and can design geometric and structural parts of Railway track, analyze and design of foundations and underground structures, manage construction projects, conduct various material tests and geotechnical investigations, maintain and inspect railway infrastructures. Admission requirement for the program is based on the general application and admission policy of the Addis Ababa University and Ethiopian Ministry of Education. The applicant shall have a B.Sc. degree in Civil Engineering or its equivalent and pass an entrance examination administered by African Railway Centre of Excellence (ARCE). Foreign applicants whose native language is other than English and the medium of instruction not English, a language test for proficiency is required.

CTBE.department_name: MSc/ MEng in Biomedical Engineering (Instrumentation and Imaging)
CTBE.courses: The MSc in Biomedical Engineering (Bio-instrumentation and Imaging) is an academic program that needs two years in the regular or three years in the evening program, that focuses on diagnostic and therapeutic principles, applications, and technologies as part of a modern healthcare services. While students from academic profession join the MSc program are required to take additional Pedagogy Course and write supervised MSc theses, students from Industry are exempt both courses and instead take two additional courses and supervised Industry Project. The primary admission requirement for the program is BSc in Biomedical Engineering from a recognized university and pass in national higher education entrance examination as well entrance examination set by the Department. Applicants with BSc in Electrical Engineering and Applied Physics are also admitted based on performance on bridge courses in two semesters.

CTBE.department_name: MSc/ MEng in Biomedical Engineering (Biomechanics and Rehabilitation)
CTBE.courses: The MSc in Biomedical Engineering (Bio-mechanics and Rehabilitation) is an academic program that needs two years in the regular or three years in the evening program, that focuses on bio-mechanical principles, applications, and technologies for rehabilitation of injured or natural health problems. While students from academic profession join the MSc program are required to take additional Pedagogy Course and write supervised MSc theses, students from Industry are exempt both courses and instead take two additional courses and supervised Industry Project. The primary admission requirement for the program is BSc in Biomedical Engineering from a recognized university and pass in national higher education entrance examination as well entrance examination set by the Department. Applicants with BSc in Mechanical Engineering and Applied Physics are also admitted based on performance on bridge courses in two semesters. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for equivalency to local examination results.

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Communication Engineering
CTBE.courses: The M.Sc. in Communication Engineering program delves into the core principles and cutting-edge applications of communication systems. It enables gain a deep understanding of: Signal Processing: Master the techniques for manipulating and analyzing signals – the lifeblood of communication – to improve transmission quality and extract information. Communication Networks: Explore the architecture and design of networks such as cellular systems, fiber optics, and the internet, learning how data travels across vast distances. Error Correction and Coding: Unravel the secrets of ensuring data integrity during transmission. You'll learn about techniques to combat noise and errors, guaranteeing reliable communication. Wireless Communication Systems: Dive into the world of radio waves and cellular networks, understanding how information travels through the air. You'll explore advanced technologies like 5G and beyond. Communication Security: Learn about the crucial aspects of data protection and network security in today's connected world. Applicants for the MSc in Communication Engineering program must hold a BSc degree with a minimum of 42 ECTS in core courses related to Communication engineering. Admission to the program also requires passing the school’s entrance exam as well as the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by the University. For applicants with foreign qualifications, examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education to confirm their equivalency to the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE).

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Electrical Power Engineering
CTBE.courses: Electrical Power Engineering is a field focused on the generation, transmission, distribution, and utilization of electrical power. Everything in our lives depends on electrical power from cooking food, information exchange, healthcare facility, agriculture. Therefore, electrical power engineers work on everything from power plants to smart grids, ensuring that electricity reaches homes, businesses, and industries reliably and efficiently. The M.Sc. in Electrical Power Engineering program aims at Equipping graduates with the knowledge and skills necessary for successful careers in electrical power generation, electrical power transmission, electrical power distribution, electrical power monitoring and electrical power protection Preparing students to address real-world electrical power engineering problems through a strong foundation in theory and practical application. Fostering innovation and creativity to design and develop new renewable electrical power generation, transmission, distribution, monitoring, control and protection technologies with societal impact. Developing well-rounded engineers with strong communication and potentially managerial skills, allowing them to effectively collaborate and lead in their chosen field. Applicants for the MSc in Electrical power Engineering program must hold a BSc degree with a minimum of 42 ECTS in core courses related to Electrical Power engineering. Admission to the program also requires passing the school’s entrance exam as well as the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by the University. For applicants with foreign qualifications, examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education to confirm their equivalency to the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE).

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Computer Engineering
CTBE.courses: Computer Engineering is a specialized field that combines the principles of electrical engineering and computer science. It focuses on the design, development, and implementation of computer hardware and software systems. Key areas of focus in computer engineering include: Hardware Design: Creating the physical components of computers, including microprocessors, memory chips, and other electronic devices. Software Development: Designing, writing, and testing software applications and operating systems that run on computer hardware. Computer Architecture: Understanding the underlying structure and organization of computers, including their components and how they interact. Computer Networks: Developing and managing networks that connect computers and other devices, enabling communication and data sharing.Embedded Systems: Designing computer systems that are embedded in other devices, such as smartphones, cars, and appliances. The M.Sc. in Computer Engineering is designed to address the evolving technological landscape and meet the specific needs of Ethiopia's growing technology sector. The program aims to cultivate highly skilled professionals who will contribute to technological innovation and industrial development. Applicants for the MSc in Computer Engineering program must hold a BSc degree with a minimum of 42 ECTS in core courses related to Computer engineering. Admission to the program also requires passing the school’s entrance exam as well as the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by the University. For applicants with foreign qualifications, examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education to confirm their equivalency to the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE).

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Control Engineering
CTBE.courses: Control Engineering is a branch of engineering that deals with the design, construction, and operation of systems that control the behavior of other systems. It involves the application of control theory to analyze and design systems that regulate, guide, or direct the behavior of other systems. Control engineers play a crucial role in various industries, including aerospace, automotive, robotics, and manufacturing. They are responsible for designing and implementing control systems for aircraft, robots, manufacturing processes, and other complex systems. Graduates of the MSc in Control Engineering will possess the following profile: Advanced Analytical and Design Skills: Skilled in understanding and modeling the dynamic behavior of physical systems, including mechanical, electrical, thermal, and fluid systems. Industrial Control Systems: Understanding and supervision of modern industrial control equipment, including Distributed Control Systems (DCS), Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA), and Programmable Logic Controllers (PLC). Automation and Innovation: Ability to play a pivotal role in the automation of industries, contributing to the enhancement of industrial processes through advanced control technologies. Autonomous Systems and Intelligent Systems: Proficiency in designing and implementing autonomous systems, including autonomous vehicles, robotics, and unmanned aerial vehicles (UAVs). Skilled in developing intelligent control systems that leverage artificial intelligence and machine learning for advanced decision-making and problem-solving. Applicants for the MSc in Control Engineering program must hold a BSc degree with a minimum of 42 ECTS in core courses related to Control engineering. Admission to the program also requires passing the school’s entrance exam as well as the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by the University. For applicants with foreign qualifications, examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education to confirm their equivalency to the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE)

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Control and Automation (Sugar Engineering)
MSc in Industrial Engineering (with Systems Engineering and Technology track and Innovation Management track)
MSc in Mechanical Engineering (Manufacturing Engineering)
CTBE.courses: MSc in Mechanical Engineering(Manufacturing Engineering) Manufacturing Engineering MSc program is intended to play a great role on enhancing traditional manufacturing, participating on introducing modern and up-to-date manufacturing areas for enabling the country to achieve the highest expected GDP from the sector. The plan include increasing the capacity utilization of manufacturing industries from 50% to 85%, promoting the market share of locally manufactured industrial products from 30% to 60%, and securing a total of five million jobs over the ten-year period. The program includes practical expertise part of Engineering in academia for transforming Production, Product Quality and sustainability with the integration of digital enhancement and industrial robotics in the Enterprise systems. Students are exposed to fabrication, technology selection and operation of Basic Metal, Engineering and Process industry systems, in Casting, traditional and modern Machining operation output and Production equipment and Enterprise systems; Modular design and Fabrication of industrial processes equipment; design and performance analysis composite material; more over digital manufacturing, smart manufacturing system and advanced manufacturing with appropriate tools. It is expected to play a great role in the basic metal engineering on design and fabrication of Tools and Dies, in engineering and process industries on developing & manufacturing of optimized equipment and interfacing of industrial robotics for sustainable product repeatability. Manufacturing engineers play a crucial role in ensuring the smooth and efficient operation of the production process, automation of production system including CNC system, while also driving continuous improvement and innovation to enhance the company's competitiveness and profitability of manufacturing industries. The Graduate are expected to be enterprise leader of local Manufacturing and Process industries and Entrepreneur who enables to interface with the rapid growth of the knowledge to get along with the furious competitive global market of the sectoral landscape. The admission requirements include the following points: The candidates for Manufacturing Engineering program must have BSc./B.Tech. Degree in Industrial, Mechanical, Production, Manufacturing Technology/ Engineering and meet other requirements set by the School of Graduate Studies. Who can join the program? The program is intended to benefit a wide range of candidates, in particular: Established manufacturing engineers working in industry and faced with the challenge of new areas of responsibility. Managers and product designers working in manufacturing organizations that need to invest in their personal career development. Engineering and Technology professionals/graduates from Mechanical, Industrial, Manufacturing, Production and Material. Recent engineering and technology professionals/graduates who have decided to move into manufacturing and related disciplines. Specific Requirements i. Screening Examination and Interview A candidate seeking admission into a MSc program must pass the screening entrance examination as well as the interview. The screening examinations will be prepared based on courses of higher degree which Manufacturing Engineering graduates are expected to have pursued. ii. Graduate Admission Test (GAT) Exam Requirement MSc Curriculum in Manufacturing Engineering (SMiE – AAiT – AAU) 20 A candidate to the MSc in Manufacturing Engineering program must meet the minimum GAT exam score requirement for admission. This requirement is typically based on the AAU’s assessment of the necessary academic aptitude and preparation for successful completion of the doctoral program. The specific GAT score threshold may vary from year to year and can be obtained from the program's admission guidelines or by contacting the AAU directly

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Mechanical Engineering (Thermal Engineering)
CTBE.courses: M.Sc. in Mechanical Engineering (Thermal Engineering) The M.Sc. in Mechanical Engineering (Thermal Engineering) is a specialized program designed to equip students with advanced knowledge and skills in thermal engineering. The program emphasizes the design, analysis, and optimization of thermal systems, including energy conversion machines, HVAC systems, and industrial processes. Students will develop expertise in enhancing energy efficiency and minimizing environmental impact across industries such as power generation, automotive, aerospace, and manufacturing. The curriculum integrates cutting-edge technologies like AI, digital twins, and advanced computational tools, preparing graduates to address complex engineering challenges. Additionally, the program incorporates emerging technologies, such as data center cooling, smart turbomachinery design, and the optimization of selected thermal equipment, to ensure that students are at the forefront of industry advancements. Graduates of the program will be highly skilled professionals ready to contribute to both industry and research. They will be proficient in designing and optimizing thermal systems, conducting energy audits, and implementing sustainable practices. With a strong foundation in advanced computational methods and industry-specific applications, graduates will be equipped to tackle challenges in sectors ranging from automotive and aerospace to manufacturing and power generation. Moreover, they will possess essential 21st-century skills, including critical thinking, problem-solving, and effective communication, making them well-rounded professionals capable of driving innovation and excellence in the field of thermal engineering. The following general and specific admission requirements shall be applied. · Selection and admission of students is done according to the working Senate Legislation of Addis Ababa University, and if applicable, university, national policies and regulations with clear, efficient, explicit and fair process. · Applicants must pass the university’s Graduate Admission Test (GAT) · The applicant must have a B.Sc. degree in Mechanical Engineering or related field from recognized higher learning institutions

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Mechanical Engineering (Mechanical Design Engineering)
CTBE.courses: MSc Mechanical Engineering (Mechanical Design Engineering) Mechanical Design Engineering M.Sc program provides training using newly developed subjects and resources. The program equips students with the knowledge and skills of advanced materials behavior, structural design, complex mechanical systems, and research principles. It is appropriate for those who have aspired to specialize in applied mechanics research, design, analyses, optimization, product development, and product testing of mechanical systems, structures, and components. The master’s program offers a comprehensive understanding of mechanical design science and principles used in research, mechanical product design, and lectures that prepare graduates for several exciting professional paths. The program provides the core concepts of mechanical design engineering incorporates modules such as analytical and computational methods, advanced applied mechanics, advanced product design, and testing. The applicant must have: B.Sc. degree in Mechanical Engineering or equivalent from recognized higher learning institutions who meets other requirements set by the school/chair and by Graduate Studies office of Addis Ababa University. Pass an entrance examination administered by the chair(must score minimum 50% ) SPECIFIC REQUIREMENTS The chair will decide on case-by-case basis for applicants with different backgrounds and other related Disciplines. However, the chair must consider the following mandatory points: Applicant from different specialization must take minimum the following mandatory courses/modules: Engineering Mechanics I-Statics, Engineering Mechanics II-Dynamics, Strength of Materials I & II, Engineering Materials I & II, Machine Elements I & II, Mechanisms of Machinery, Mechanical Vibration, Machine Design Project I & II, Tribology, Introduction to Mechatronics, and Instrumentation and Measurement, Manufacturing Engineering and Product Design If there is a difference on the course/module name with the course/module listed above, the applicant must submit a certified detail course/module description from his previous university. If the applicant full fill all the requirement set by the university and the entire requirement indicated on this curriculum except lack of taking some of the above listed mandatory courses/modules for program he/she intended to attend, the applicant will be admitted by allowing him/her to take a bridging courses/module with the undergraduate student. However, the maximum number of bridging course/modules will be three.

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Chemical Engineering (Process Engineering)
CTBE.courses: The Master's program (MSc) in Process Engineering is a regular two-year program offered at the School of Chemical and Bioengineering, Addis Ababa Institute of Technology, Addis Ababa University. This program aims to equip students with advanced knowledge in all aspects of chemical-related process engineering and technology over the course of three semesters. In the fourth semester, students engage in rigorous scientific research, gaining expertise in their chosen interdisciplinary areas and developing competencies through a research project and thesis within the framework of chemical engineering. By providing essential qualifications in the increasingly multicultural field of process engineering on a global scale, this program prepares students to play a crucial role in the development of new materials, sustainable energy systems, and chemical conversion technologies. These advancements aim to mitigate climate change and reduce pollution by optimizing and developing processes across diverse sectors in the chemical industry. To be admitted into the program, applicants must have completed a satisfactory bachelor's degree at a recognized higher education institution, as well as pass the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by the University. For applicants who have completed foreign examinations, their results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for equivalency

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Chemical Engineering (Environmental Engineering)
CTBE.courses: MSc in Environmental Engineering is a two-year regular graduate program that studies advanced theories and practices in environmental engineering. Students will study root causes of environmental (industrial/mining, agricultural, municipal, etc.) pollutions and learn state-of-the-art engineering methods to solve them based on principles of sustainability, zero waste, circular economy, life cycle thinkings. The program has sufficient flexibility for students to choose courses based on their research focus area. The study will produce highly competent and professionals of high caliber that respond better to the capacity building needs and sustainable development of the nation. Admission requirement for the program is based on the general graduate admission requirements of AAU and the GAT (Graduate Admission Test) results offered by the Ministry of Education.

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Chemical Engineering (Food Engineering)
CTBE.courses: MSc. in Food Engineering is a two-year regular program aimed to equip students with advanced theoretical and practical knowledge in Food Process Engineering and Technology, which includes in depth exploration of food process engineering principles, developing strategies and methods for sustainable food production systems,  designing methods for efficient and effective food processing system and all other essential  skills and knowledge in the field of food process engineering.  In the second year, students mainly work on rigorous scientific research that help them receive expertise and achieve competencies through hands on experiments and data analysis in the area of food process  engineering. To be admitted to the program students must have completed BSc degree from recognized university in Food Engineering/related fields of study with a minimum CGPA of 2.0. Additionally, the students should pass Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by the University (Addis Ababa University) and program related examination offered by the School of Chemical and Bio Engineering, Addis Ababa Institute of Technology, Addis Ababa University.

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Chemical Engineering (Industrial Biotechnology and Biochemical Engineering)
CTBE.courses: The Master of Science (MSc) in Industrial Biotechnology and Biochemical Engineering program at the School of Chemical and Bio Engineering, AAiT, aims to equip graduates with the skills and knowledge to apply biotechnology and biochemical engineering principles to revolutionize industrial bioprocesses. The program covers critical topics such as biofuel production, bioplastics, pharmaceuticals, food and beverages, and other valuable products. It also focuses on utilizing renewable biological resources to reduce reliance on fossil fuels, decrease greenhouse gas emissions, and minimize waste. The program is ideal for graduates with a B.Sc. degree in engineering or non-engineering fields. Applicants must pass the National/University Graduate Admission Test (GAT) to demonstrate proficiency in basic communication, arithmetic, and logical analysis skills. Candidates with non-engineering educational backgrounds requiring bridges will undergo a course auditing process to address any gaps in foundational knowledge necessary for the MSc program.

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Water Resources Engineering and Management
CTBE.courses: The MSc Program in Water Resource Engineering and Management (WREM) is a two-year program. Students in this program will have an in-depth understanding of the current theories and concepts in surface and subsurface hydrology, the relevant physical, chemical and biological process interactions between the hydrosphere, the lithosphere, the biosphere and the atmosphere, and have a thorough awareness of the natural and human-induced variability in space and time of hydrological systems. They will also be expected to apply and integrate the relevant physical, chemical, applied mathematical, computational and earth-scientific principles and concepts, and to use information and communication technology within a hydrological context. The MSc Program in Water Resource Engineering and Management (WREM) has the following two tracks. . MSc in Water Resource Engineering and Management (Hydrology and Water Resources Management) MSc students who specialize in Hydrology and Water Resource Management will master the major hydrological methodologies and applications with regard to both water quantity and water quality, including techniques for data collection, processing and analysis, and the application of catchment hydrological modeling, impacts of climate change and aquifer modeling techniques, remote sensing and GIS. . MSc in Water Resource Engineering and Management (Irrigation Engineering and Management) Students who specialize in Irrigation Engineering and Management will master the irrigation engineering and management methodologies and applications with irrigation design, irrigation water management, soil water management and irrigation scheduling as well the hydrological methodologies. Applicant to either of the MSc in Water Resource Engineering and Management tracks must have a BSc Degree in Water Resources Engineering, Hydraulic Engineering, Civil Engineering, Irrigation Engineering, Water Supply and Sanitary Engineering, Hydrogeology, Geology, Geography, Environmental Engineering or other related areas from a recognized university

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Geomatics Engineering
CTBE.courses: The MSc graduate program in Geomatics Engineering would thus offer advanced instruction in pure and applied sciences and engineering, with emphasis on basic and applied engineering sciences and involving students in research activities that aim to identify and address Geomatics Engineering problems in line with the Country’s development needs. Studies in Geomatics Engineering include topics on Mathematical Methods, Engineering Surveying, Land Administration, Spatial Databases, Computer Programming, Adjustment Theory, Satellite Positioning, Photogrammetry, Geographic Information Systems, Cartography, Remote Sensing, Deformation Monitoring, Physical Geodesy, and Reference Systems and Map Projection. Admission requirement for candidates entering this program must have obtained Bachelor degree in surveying, civil engineering, geomatics engineering, or its equivalent and meet the admission requirements of the school of Graduate Studies. He/she must also pass the entrance examination administered by the school. Regarding applicants from other related disciplines, the School Graduate Committee (SGC), will rule on the eligibility of such candidates. Additional skills in computational mathematics and computer skills (particularly programming) will be additional asset to students. The School Academic Council (SAC) will decide on a case-by-case basis for applicants who do not satisfy the first admission requirement

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Civil Engineering (Geotechnical Engineering)
CTBE.courses: MSc in Geotechnical Engineering is a two-year and three-year regular and extension program in respective order that studies the basic principles of science and their application to the field of geotechnical engineering. It will provide a solid background in all areas of geotechnical engineering and helps students to concentrate on areas of particular interest in the areas of soil mechanics, foundation engineering, geotechnical earthquake engineering, dynamic analysis of geotechnical structures, landslides (assessments and remedies), slope stability, embankment dam, retaining structures (conventional ones, shoring structures, …), machine foundations, geotechnical investigation and monitoring (planning and execution), soil-structure interaction, and physical and numerical modeling. Students will be trained with advanced methods of analysis so that efficient design of geotechnical engineering structures in terms of safety and economy will be made. Students will work closely with faculty and also interact with practitioners through field trips, seminars and professional symposiums and meetings. Admission requirement for the program requires a pass result of Graduate Aptitude Test (GAT) administered by the university or another pertinent governmental body, such as the ministry of education, in order to evaluate their verbal, quantitative, and analytical reasoning skills as indicators of potential success in graduate studies, two letters of recommendation, a written statement of purpose, curriculum vitae, BSc degree in Civil Engineering, Civil and Environmental Engineering or a closely related discipline, a pass result of entrance examination designed by the school of Civil engineering, covering relevant coursework in the undergraduate program like hydraulics, soil mechanics, foundation engineering, mechanics of materials, reinforced concrete

CTBE.department_name: MSc in Civil Engineering (Road and Transport Engineering) [with track in transport and traffic Engineering and traffic safety system track]

CELS.courses: BA in Chinese Language: The BA in Chinese Language is a four-year regular program that offers a comprehensive exploration of Chinese language and culture, aiming to cultivate the professionals in the field with good mastery of the Chinese language, good understanding of Chinese culture and good operation of the cross-culture communication skills. Students studying Chinese progress from an elementary working knowledge of the language to oral and written fluency. The rich body of Chinese culture is composed of the Chinese society and life, essence of traditional Chinese culture and modern China. The culture part is first studied in English; as students’ progress in the major and develops their language skills, they continue in Chinese. The qualified graduates can be recommended to apply for the scholarship to study in China. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE.

CELS.department_name: BA in Ethiopian Sign Language and Interpreting
CELS.courses: BA in Ethiopian Sign Language and Interpretation The BA in Ethiopian Sign Language and Interpretation is a four-year regular program designed to immerse students in Ethiopian Sign Language (EthSL), Deaf culture, and sign language interpretation. The curriculum begins with foundational courses in EthSL grammar, vocabulary, and usage, progressing to advanced interpretation skills for various settings such as educational institutions, government organizations, and NGOs. Students will also engage in research and documentation of EthSL and learn to provide consultancy services, manage educational and rehabilitation programs, and develop early intervention strategies for Deaf children. Admission requirements for the program include results of the National College Entrance Examination, general criteria set by Addis Ababa University or the Ministry of Education, and certification of foreign examination results for equivalency. Priority is given to Deaf applicants

CELS.department_name: BA in Amharic Language, Literature and Folklore
CELS.courses: BA in Amharic Language, Literature, and Folklore The BA in Amharic Language, Literature, and Folklore is a comprehensive four-year program designed to immerse students in the rich linguistic and cultural heritage of Ethiopia and its scientific study. This program offers a diverse curriculum that encompasses the study of the Amharic language, its literature, and the vibrant folklore that reflects the traditions and values of the peoples of Ethiopia at large. Students will develop a deep understanding of the language's structure and nuances, acquire professional skills in its varied applications, while also exploring the major literary works and the lively materials under the four forms of folklore. Through engaging coursework and practical experiences, students will be equipped with the necessary skills to analyse texts, conduct research, and appreciate the cultural contexts of Amharic literature and folklore. Graduates of this program will be well-prepared for various career paths, including roles as language researchers, literary critics, editors, translators, cultural experts, public relations officers, and course material developers as well aseducators in university settings. The program emphasizes critical thinking, effective communication, and cultural competency, ensuring that students are not only proficient in the Amharic language but also capable of contributing to the preservation and promotion of Ethiopia's literary and cultural heritage. Admission to the program is based on the results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the student's preferences, and the current capacity of the department, along with the UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) conducted by the University. Foreign examination results must be authenticated by the Ministry of Education for equivalency to the EHEECE

CELS.department_name: BA in Oromo Literature and Communication
CELS.courses: BA in Afan Oromo, Literature and Communication The BA in Afan Oromo, Literature, and Communication is a four-year degree program designed to provide students with comprehensive knowledge and skills in the Oromo language, literature, folklore, and communication. The curriculum encompasses these diverse fields and aims to promote the development of Oromo language, culture, and literature while also preserving and enriching Oromo heritage and social customs through education and research. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE

CELS.department_name: BA in English Language and Literature
CELS.courses: BA in English Language and Literature The BA in English Language and Literature is a four-year study program designed to equip students with comprehensive skills and knowledge, preparing them for the modern job market. Students learn how human language functions and the principles guiding its scientific study, while also practicing listening, speaking, reading, and writing in English. The curriculum emphasizes key areas: vocabulary, grammar, literature, and linguistics, including English phonology, morphology, syntax, semantics, discourse, and pragmatics. The program's delivery mode fosters practical language skills, enabling students to apply their knowledge in various professional contexts. They engage in oral communication, analyze literary texts, and conduct research, ultimately mastering the language for confident, effective use in their careers. The curriculum aims to produce graduates who are well-prepared for roles in public relations, communications, interpretation, and other fields where English language expertise is in demand. The following are admission requirements for the Bachelor of Arts Degree in English Language and Literature: Applicants must meet the general admission requirements set by the Ministry of Education (MoE) for undergraduate programs in Ethiopian universities. Applicants must have successfully completed the preparatory program and passed the university entrance examination. A strong background in English is required, as demonstrated by high grades in English courses during secondary education. Candidates should have a demonstrated interest in literature, language studies, and communication. Transfer students from other recognized universities may be considered for admission, provided they meet the specific departmental requirements and have completed relevant coursework. Students or those with equivalent qualifications may be admitted upon passing the university’s entrance exam and fulfilling any additional requirements set by the department. Candidates may be required to attend an interview or submit a personal statement outlining their interest in the program and future career aspirations.

CELS.department_name: BA in Arabic Language and Communication
CELS.courses: BA in Arabic Language and Communication The BA in Arabic Language and Communication is a four-year regular study program. The program focuses on equipping students with basic Arabic language in order to raise the students’ language skills to a good level of proficiency in Arabic Language before getting into the analysis and the uses of the language in itself. Furthermore, the program will focus more on professional skills that will eventually orient graduating students to a professional life. In order to join the program, the students must fulfill the following requirements: Successful passing of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE). High interest in the field. The capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Be willing to attend any entrance exam that the Unit may have to prepare. Students who have no prior knowledge of Arabic will be admitted into the second year; Holders of bachelors in other fields from an Arabic speaking country recognized by the Ministry of Education could be admitted into the third year of the BA Program. Applicants, who graduated from local institutions including holders of a diploma or a degree in another field of study, who have prior Knowledge in Arabic, could take an Arabic language test administered by the Arabic Unit of the Addis Ababa University. Those who would pass this test could be admitted into the third year of the BA Program. Those who fail the examination could be accepted as second year students

Our BSc in Exercise and Sports Science undergraduate program is crafted to provide students with the flexibility to pursue their interests through five specialized concentration areas. Students will delve into athletic performance science, address preventive and rehabilitative aspects of sports, and promote health and wellness through exercise. The program also covers coaching techniques and the management of sports organizations. With hands-on internship opportunities and a senior essay project focused on their specialization, students are well-prepared to embark on successful careers in the diverse field of Exercise and Sports Science. Admission to the BSc in Exercise and Sport Science program is based on Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE) results, student preferences, departmental capacity, and Undergraduate Admission Test (UGAT) scores. Applicants must undergo a fitness and medical assessment. Following Article 81.1 and upcoming Senate guidelines, previous Ethiopian elite athletes may be admitted under the department's dual career policy. Foreign examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for EHEECE equivalency.

The Bachelor of Education in Physical Education is a four-year program that combines theoretical knowledge with practical experience. Students learn about individual sports like gymnastics and self-defense, team sports such as football and volleyball, and essential scientific concepts including exercise physiology and athletic injury prevention. The program includes three practicum courses for hands-on training and emphasizes technology and pedagogy to prepare future educators. Additionally, students conduct action research to explore topics of interest, helping them become well-rounded teachers in the field. Admission for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Secondary School Leaving Certificate Examination (ESSLCE), the interest of the student, performance test and the capacity of the department/School/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the ESSLCE.

CELS.department_name: BEd in Civics and Ethical Education
CELS.courses: The BEd in Civics and Ethical Education is designed for four-year regular program to provide the students of Civic and Ethical Education with detailed knowledge on key concepts; namely; society, state, government, Political Philosophy, the major theories and schools of Moral thought, fundamental of human rights and humanitarian Assistance, Constitution and how constitution is made and implemented is the basic crux/root of the study of rights and duties of citizens, federalism and multicultural systems, Conflict and conflict resolution, to be equipped with fundamentals of public administration and public law, concepts, theories and strategies related to development. The program also address educators, teachers and other personnel engaged in preparing or designing curricula, textbooks and teaching materials also need to be informed of current theories, principles and methods necessary to deal with variegated networks of problems and opportunities that a multilingual and multicultural society is often confronted with. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE

CELS.department_name: BEd in Special Needs and Inclusive Education
CELS.courses: Bachelor of Art in Special Needs and Inclusive Education Bachelor of Art in Special Needs and Inclusive Education is a four years undergraduate program. Special needs education is to address the right to education of marginalized groups of learners special needs such persons with disabilities and other vulnerable segments of the society, taking their special educational needs in to account through facilitating them to learn in accordance with their potential and needs. Addis Ababa University is a pioneer in launching Bachelor of Art degree in Special Needs and Inclusive Education in 2006. More than 1000 students have graduated from the Department are now working in Universities, Colleges, NGOS, schools, International Institutions such as UNESO, UNICEF and work as a consultant, self employed. The program is internationally recognized and has created many job opportunities for our former graduates. The Bachelor degree in Special Needs and Inclusive Education leads the candidate to MA program and then PhD. The Department is very rich in human and material resources. Two full professors, three associate professors and three assistance professors involves in the BA program. There is well organized resource centre for BA students to learn from it. The Department has very strong link with international universities such as Munich University (Germany), Jyvaskyla University (Finland), and Vienna University (Austria) to share international experience for our students. There are also some other inward international collaborations. In order to be admitted the program applicants should complete grade 12 and achieve the requirement as per the Admission, Recruitment and Placement policy of Addis Ababa University or hold diploma or degree in any field of studies.

The four-year undergraduate program in Educational Planning and Management will produce graduates working in the sector of education in educational management positions. The program intends to equip graduates with the necessary scientific and practical knowledge, skills and attitudes so as to help them solve existing problems of Ethiopian education system. The program is aimed to produce democratic, visionary, innovative, change oriented and professionally committed educational planners and managers with major competencies such as planning, management, leadership, monitoring, evaluation, research, technology use who can hold leadership responsibilities under decentralized educational contexts. English language is also taught as a minor course for the trainers. Admission to the regular BA program in Educational Planning and Management requires ssuccessful completion of freshman program (general education courses) set by the university or having at least diploma in educational management or related teaching subjects with a minimum cumulative grade point average (CGPA) of 2:00 at graduation from an accredited Teacher training institution.Teaching or administrative experiences in educational organizations will be an advantage to get admission to the program.

CELS.department_name: MA in Education leadership and Management (four concentration areas: Educational Planning and Project Management, Higher Education Management and Policy, Supervision, and Human
CELS.courses: The MA in Educational Leadership and Managementis being offered in a regular, extension, summer, and via distance supported with online along with face-to-face interaction (blended) for the duration of 2 - 3 years depending on the mode of deliveries. The program aimed at preparing self-motivated, and socially responsible educational policy analysts, planners, managers/leaders, and researchers in the public and private sectors.It tries to familiarize learners with concepts and practical applications of educational leadership and management at the levels of general education, TVET, higher education, adult education, and preschool. In journey of the course, learners will be equipped with the skills to diagnose the education sector using EMIS data. This program is structured under four tracks or concentration areas in Educational Planning and Project Management, Higher Education Management and Policy, Supervision, and Human Resources and Organizational Development (HROD). To get admission for master’s degree program in Educational Leadership and Management, the applicants must have a bachelor’s degree with a CGPA of 2.0 and above; successfully pass the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) offered by the University; and fulfill other university wide requirements

CELS.department_name: MA in Teaching Chinese to Speakers of Other Languages (MTCSOL)
CELS.courses: MA in Teaching Chinese to Speakers of Other Languages (TCSOL) The MA in Teaching Chinese to Speakers of Other Languages is a two-year regular program that studies the basic theories of pedagogy, second language acquisition, language education research, cross-culture communication and their application to the field of teaching Chinese to Speakers of Other languages. Students will receive the training in both the Chinese language and culture and the skills of teaching and research, laying a firm foundation for the profession development as an international Chinese language teacher. The qualified MA year 1 students can have scholarship opportunities to continue the MA second-year study in China. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as GAT (Graduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the degree level requested by Addis Ababa University.

CVMA.department_name: Bachelor of Degree in Veterinary Science (BVSc)
CVMA.courses: The Bachelor of Veterinary Sciences (BVSc) is a four-year regular program. The BVSc program with three different tracks (Animal Health, Veterinary Public Health and Veterinary Laboratory Technology) is designed to prepare graduates with the knowledge, skills, and attitudes to provide safe and competent animal health care. The objective of the BVSc program is to produce competent animal health, veterinary public health and laboratory technology practitioners who would provide the highest standard animal health, veterinary public health and veterinary laboratory services to meet the needs and aspiration of the country, region and the world. A graduate of any of the three BVSc tracks can advance to a DVM (professional focus) or MSc degree after taking bridge courses based on program’s requirements. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE) and UGAT (Under-Graduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Generally, the criteria set by the Ministry of Education (MoE) for admission will be applied to full-time regular students. Students with diploma in animal health, biology, or animal sciences with a minimum cGPA of 2.00 and above can also be admitted with some course exemptions following course equivalence calculations and/or course audits based on academic commission recommendations.

CVMA.department_name: MSc in Food Security and Development
CVMA.courses: MSc Food Security and Development The MSc program in Food Security and Development at Addis Ababa University's Center for Food Security Studies addresses Ethiopia's food security challenges through a multidisciplinary approach that combines advanced research, policy analysis, and practical solutions. It is designed to develop highly skilled professionals capable of making significant contributions to national and regional food security efforts. The program focuses on training experts for policy advisory, research, and consultancy roles, advancing food security through impactful research, promoting scholarly and policy discussions, and providing technical advice and consultancy services. Register now to be part of the solution to Ethiopia’s food security challenges! Applicants for the program must meet general and specific requirements to be admitted. General requirements include adhering to Addis Ababa University’s affirmative action policies, providing an equivalence letter for degrees obtained abroad, and fulfilling all other university admission criteria. Specific requirements include holding a first degree from an accredited institution in relevant fields, having a minimum overall CGPA of 2.50 and 2.75 in the major on a 4.00 scale, submitting two recommendation letters, a two-page letter of intent, and preferably having work experience in food security and development. Additionally, candidates must pass selection tests and the Graduate Admission Test (GAT)

CVMA.department_name: MSc in Tropical Infectious Diseases, Vet Epidemiology and Public Health
CVMA.courses: The Master of Science (MSc) in Tropical Infectious Diseases, Veterinary Epidemiology, and Public Health is a two-year graduate program offering three specialized tracks: (1) Veterinary Epidemiology, (2) Veterinary Public Health, and (3) Tropical Infectious Diseases. The program equips graduates with advanced competencies in several key areas, including epidemiology, infectious diseases prevalent in tropical regions, food safety, foodborne diseases, economic evaluation of animal health and food safety, and best practices for handling animals and animal products. Candidates eligible for admission to this program must possess recognized degrees in biomedical fields, particularly in Veterinary Medicine (DVM), Medical Doctor (MD), Bachelor of Veterinary Science (BVSc), or related disciplines, and pass the Graduate Admission Test GAT) and subject specific exams as per the regulations of Addis Ababa University's graduate programs.

CVMA.department_name: MSc in Veterinary Microbiology and Parasitology
CVMA.courses: The MSc in Veterinary Microbiology and Parasitology program is a two-year postgraduate program that focuses on the study of microbial and parasitic pathogens of veterinary and zoonotic importance. The program begins by reviewing the basics and then progresses to explaining the molecular and genetic basis of animal pathogen diversity. It also covers the interaction of pathogens with their hosts and vectors, as well as the development and use of diagnostic tools, control methods, and preventive approaches. This program is jointly offered by the Department of Microbiology, Immunology and Veterinary Public Health and the Department of Pathology and Parasitology. It offers two tracks: the Veterinary Microbiology track and the Veterinary Parasitology track, and students are placed into these tracks based on their preferences.
