Applicants should have a first degree in animal health or a related field. Candidates from other biological science disciplines may also be considered on a conditional basis. Admission to this MSc program depends on the applicant's preference, the department's student intake capacity, the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) provided by Addis Ababa University, and a program-specific entrance exam conducted by the department offering the program.

CVMA.department_name: MSc in Poultry Health and Management
CVMA.courses: The MSc in Poultry Health and Management program is a two-year postgraduate program that focuses on the overall health and management of poultry in both commercial and backyard production systems. It offers conceptual and practical training in the management of commercial poultry farms, covering everything from poultry feeding and breeding to poultry product processing. The program also has a strong emphasis on poultry health and biosecurity, including disease risk assessment, emergency preparedness, disease outbreak and agent identification, and the implementation of effective preventive and control measures. The program is offered by the Department of Pathology and Parasitology, with significant contributions from the Departments of Animal Production Studies and Microbiology, Immunology and Veterinary Public Health. Applicants should possess a first degree in Animal Sciences or a related animal health field. Candidates from other biological sciences disciplines may be considered on a conditional basis. Admission to this MSc program is determined by the applicant's preference, the department's student intake capacity, the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) administered by Addis Ababa University, and a program-specific entrance examination conducted by the department offering the program.

The Master of Science (MVSc) in Veterinary Clinical Sciences is a two-year graduate program that offers four specialized tracks: (1) Veterinary Surgery, (2) Clinical Medicine, (3) Theriogenology, and (4) Veterinary Pathology. This program encompasses a variety of courses focusing on advanced clinical techniques, research methodologies, and specialized veterinary topics. To be eligible for admission, candidates must hold a recognized degree in Veterinary Medicine (DVM) or a Bachelor of Veterinary Science (BVSc) in animal health-related fields. Additionally, those with a Bachelor of Science (BSc) in Veterinary Laboratory Technology (VLT) may apply for the Theriogenology and Pathology specialties, but are not eligible for Clinical Medicine or Surgery. Admission also requires passing the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) as per the regulations of Addis Ababa University's graduate programs

CVMA.department_name: MSc in Veterinary Biotechnology with focus on Animal Health
CVMA.courses: MSc in Veterinary Biotechnology Sciences is a two-year regular program that provides a comprehensive understanding of the principles of innovative biotechnology applied to veterinary medicine. This program equips students with advanced knowledge and skills to apply cutting-edge biotechnology tools to address critical challenges in animal health and production, such as animal disease diagnostics and therapeutics, animal production and reproduction, genetic improvements, and biological-based therapeutic products design and development. Students will receive a foundational knowledge of cutting-edge biotechnology techniques, including molecular biology, genetic engineering, bioinformatics, multiomics technology, and immunology. These techniques can be applied to veterinary medicine, enabling disease diagnosis, the development of vaccines and therapeutics, improvement of animal breeding, and the enhancement of animal welfare. Additionally, students will acquire hands-on experience in research and innovation through laboratory work, data analysis and scientific writing. Finally, they will also acquire essential skills for successful careers in academic research, feed, pharmaceutical and biotechnology companies, governmental agencies, and animal health organizations. Admission requirement for the program is based on Addis Ababa University’s rules and regulations governing graduate programs. The program is open to candidates with doctor of veterinary medicine, BVSc or BSc in veterinary sciences or Vet/medical laboratory technology, or BSc in biotechnology, animal sciences, biological sciences, life sciences or health-related disciplines. Candidates are required to pass the graduate admission test (GAT) and university entrance examination.

CVMA.department_name: MSc in Veterinary Pharmacology and Toxicology
CVMA.courses: MSc in Veterinary Pharmacology and Toxicology is a two-year regular program that provides a comprehensive understanding of the principles of pharmacology and toxicology, as they apply to veterinary medicine and other health sciences. It equips students with advanced knowledge and skills that are required for pursuing diverse career paths in research, academia, drug development, regulatory affairs, or veterinary clinical practice. Students will receive a foundational knowledge in general pharmacology, cellular and molecular toxicology, veterinary pharmacotherapeutics, drug discovery and development, and other supportive fields that enable them to address key veterinary issues including the use of antimicrobial and alternatives in food animals, veterinary drug efficacy and safety, antimicrobial resistance, and environmental toxicants with multidisciplinary approaches. Students will also receive hands-on laboratory experiences and conduct original research, contributing to the advancement of knowledge in the field. Admission requirement for the program is based on Addis Ababa University’s rules and regulations governing graduate programs. The program is open to candidates with a doctor of veterinary medicine (DVM), BSc or BVSc in veterinary pharmacy, veterinary sciences or Veterinary Laboratory Technology, and BSc in pharmacy, biological or chemical sciences or health-related fields. Candidates are required to pass the graduate admission test (GAT) and university entrance examination

The MSc in Animal Production is a two-year regular program that targets at promoting the scientific skills of the Gradates in terms of accomplishing tasks related to teaching, research and development in the area of livestock production. The student will be exposed to advanced courses which include Biometry, Animal Nutrition, Genetics and Animal Breeding, Poultry Production, Dairy Animals Production and Meat Animals Production. Through the practice of planning and executing research, the student is expected to be proficient in data analysis and thesis writing. In line with the Government’s expectations and the missions of the University, efforts were made to successfully produce more than 120 Graduates from the Program. Students joining the MSc program is should have BSc Degree in Animal Sciences, Veterinary Sciences, and related disciplines an equivalent Degree from an accredited university. Moreover, the applicant is expected to pass the GAT

CVMA.department_name: PhD in Food Security and Development
CVMA.courses: PhD Food Security and Development The Ph.D. program in Food Security and Development (FSD) at the Center for Food Security Studies (CFSS), College of Development Studies (CDS), Addis Ababa University (AAU), is specifically designed to tackle pivotal issues related to food security and sustainable development in Ethiopia. This program offers advanced training and research opportunities, equipping graduates with the expertise to address complex challenges in food security and development. Graduates will be prepared for influential roles in teaching, research, management, consultancy, policy advisory, and advocacy across various sectors, contributing significantly to national food security and sustainable development efforts. Applicants must meet AAU's admission criteria, holding an MA/MSc in relevant fields like development studies, food security, or disaster risk management. They must demonstrate academic excellence aligned with CDS's strategic interests and FSD priorities. Foreign degree holders must provide equivalent confirmation. Applicants must pass exams like the GAT and meet specific requirements, including a concept proposal, a preliminary research proposal, and recommendation letters. Interviews and entrance exams may be required, and candidates must demonstrate research funding and access to dissertation advisers. Previous dissertations will be evaluated if they do not align with AAU's grading system.

The PhD program in One Health tackles emerging global and local health challenges by integrating human, animal, and environmental health to enhance disease prevention, detection, and response. This program provides students with a comprehensive, multidisciplinary education, empowering

The Doctor of Philosophy (PhD) in Veterinary Sciences is a regular graduate program designed to produce highly qualified professionals across six specialized tracks: (1) Veterinary Public Health, (2) Veterinary Epidemiology, (3) Veterinary Microbiology, (4) Veterinary Parasitology, (5) Veterinary Obstetrics and Gynecology, and (6) Tropical Infectious Diseases. This PhD program aims to cultivate veterinary experts who excel in teaching, research, community service, leadership, consultancy, and policy formulation within the field of veterinary sciences, addressing the pressing issues in the livestock sector and public health challenges at both national and international levels. The curriculum includes a diverse range of courses covering topics such as Advanced One-Health and Emerging Infectious Diseases, Advanced Veterinary Food Safety Risk Analysis and Modeling, Veterinary Microbe-Host Interactions and Pathogenesis, Veterinary Microbial Genetics and Biotechnology, Advances in Animal Parasite Management, Host-Parasite Co-evolution, Infertility and Reproductive Health, Veterinary Reproductive Endocrinology, Molecular Biology and Genetics of Infectious Diseases, and Advanced Immunology of Tropical and Infectious Diseases. Applicants to the PhD program should have an MSc or MVSc degree in Veterinary Public Health, Veterinary Epidemiology, Veterinary Microbiology, Veterinary Parasitology, Veterinary Obstetrics and Gynecology, Tropical Infectious Diseases, or a related discipline, or an equivalent degree from an accredited university. Additionally, candidates are required to pass the Graduate Admission Test (GAT) and submit a synopsis of up to five pages outlining their proposed PhD research work.

The Doctor of Philosophy in Animal Production is a four-years regular program tailored to producing qualified professionals in the fields of Animal Production, Animal Nutrition or Poultry Sciences as specialization tracks. The Program is one of the most successful PhD streams in the University producing 57 PhD holders thus far. Students joining the PhD Program should have MSc or MVSc Degree in Animal Sciences, Veterinary Sciences, and related disciplines an equivalent Degree from an accredited university. Moreover, the applicant is expected to pass the GAT. The applicant is expected to submit a maximum of five pages synopsis regarding the anticipated PhD research work.

CNCS.courses: A BSc Degree in Statistics is a four-year regular program that studies the basic principles of science and their application to real-world problems. The statistics program equips students for diverse career paths by offering fundamental knowledge in probability, mathematical statistics, data analysis, and statistical computing, catering to the increasing demand for these skills in fields like big data, data science, and analytics. A BSc in Statistics opens doors to varied opportunities in business, industry, or government. For those pursuing further education in Statistics, the mathematical statistics courses prepare them well. The program emphasizes a conceptual grasp of basic probability and mathematical statistics, proficiency in data analysis and interpretation, effective communication skills, and expertise in statistical computing. Admission requirement for the program is based on the results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student, and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as the UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. or the applicant must have graduated from a recognized university/college with a diploma in Statistics or related fields. Foreign examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the local requirements.

BSc in Chemistry BSc in Chemistry is a four-year program that focuses on the study of the properties, composition, structure, reactions, and behavior of matter. It provides a comprehensive understanding of the fundamental principles and theories of chemistry. In the program, students will cover various sub disciplines of chemistry, including analytical chemistry, inorganic chemistry, organic chemistry and physical chemistry. The program consists of a combination of theoretical lectures, laboratory experiments, and practical coursework. BSc in Chemistry graduates will have a wide range of career opportunities in areas including Teaching/Academic Institutions, Chemical Industries, Research Centers, Pharmaceutical, Food and Beverage Industries, and other related manufacturing sectors. The eligibility criteria to get admission to the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the intake capacity of the Department as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE

B.Ed. in Physics is a four-year regular program designed for those candidates who desire to join the education sector through teaching physics in secondary schools. Through courses that promote a foundational grasp of physics and its application in the modern world, learners will cultivate a greater understanding and engagement with the subject matter within this program. It offers opportunity for hands-on laboratory experience, innovative exploration, demanding academics, and beneficial opportunities. Apart from imparting a firm groundwork in physics, the curriculum equips candidates with essential skills for a career in teaching, including pedagogy, evaluation, inclusive practices, and educational theory. The program's admission requirements are determined by the results of the Ethiopian Secondary School Leaving Certificate Examination (ESSLCE), the student's choice, the department's capacity, as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. For foreign applicants, foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the ESSLCE

CNCS.department_name: Bachelor of Science in Computer Science
CNCS.courses: BSc in Computer Science is a four-year regular program that studies computing systems, software development, algorithms, and the theoretical foundations of computer science. The program typically includes a courses of mathematics, programming languages, data structures, software engineering, computer architecture, databases, artificial intelligence, and operating systems. In addition to technical skills, students often learn problem-solving, critical thinking, and the ability to design and analyze complex systems. The program also includes elective courses that allow students to specialize in areas such as Cyber Security, machine learning, data science, and network security. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE.

CNCS.department_name: Bachelor of Science in Information Systems
CNCS.courses: The B.Sc. in Information Systems is a four-year program that studies a broad field that encompasses the study of how information technology (IT) is used to solve business problems and support organizational goals. The core areas that are covered in the program include, Systems Analysis and Design, Software development, Database Management, Business Process understanding, Computer Networking, Information Systems Security and Management, Project Management, and much more. Our graduates often are employed as System analyst, Database administrators, Software developers, Programmers, Network administrators, System architects, Systems designers, Business Intelligence and Knowledge Management experts, etc. Admission will be as per the AAU’s admission requirement.

The Bachelor of Science in Geology is a comprehensive four-year program designed to equip students with a deep understanding of the Earth’s processes, resources, history and environments. Through a balanced combination of theoretical knowledge, hands-on laboratory practices, and extensive fieldwork, students gain practical skills and theoretical knowledge essential for a successful career in geosciences. Students will explore the origins and evolution of our planet, the formation of minerals and rocks, and the methods used to discover and manage Earth's resources and hazards. The program includes three extensive fieldwork experiences conducted in diverse geological terrains across Ethiopia, allowing students to apply their learning in real-world settings, explore different regions, and engage with various natural and cultural landscapes. This program prepares graduates for a wide range of careers in sectors such as environmental protection, natural resource management, energy, mining, field exploration and more, making a tangible impact on Ethiopia’s development and sustainability. Applicants must meet the general university admission requirements, including the completion of the pre-college program as set by the Ministry of Education. Admission is based on the results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the student’s choice, the capacity of the School, and the Undergraduate Admission Test (UGAT) offered by the University. For foreign applicants, examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education to ensure equivalency to the EHEECE. Join us to explore the dynamic world of geology and become a leader in understanding and managing the Earth's resources and hazards.

The Biology BSc Program is a 4-year undergraduate program. It provides for students the opportunity to explore life in all complexity and diversity, and helps to develop each student’s the ability to acquire and critically interpret knowledge of basic facts and theories of biology. A strong foundation in Biology is necessary for careers in environment, agricultural productivity, industry, energy sector, biomedical sciences, climate change and teaching. Thus, attending in BSc Biology program is imperative that students be equipped with strong biological knowledge and skills which enable them to be productive. The major courses presented in the program are categorized into common, cellular biology and lab techniques, botanical sciences, zoological sciences, genetics and molecular biology, microbial and biomedical sciences, fisheries and aquatic sciences, biological research methods and applications, ecological and aquatic sciences and biotechnology modules. Additionally, basic chemistry courses and fundamentals of statistics are provided in supportive modules. In order to get admission to the program, students should successfully complete the freshman program as per the admission rules and regulations of the university and ministry of education or they must have a diploma in biology or related biological disciplines from accredited higher learning institutions

CNCS.department_name: MSc in Water Sanitation and Health
CNCS.courses: The MSc Program in Water, Sanitation and Health program is designed to equip students with the interdisciplinary expertise required to address the complex challenges of water supply, sanitation, and health. The program integrates environmental science, engineering, public health, economics, policy, and social science, fostering a comprehensive approach to managing water resources and improving sanitation and health outcomes. By understanding and addressing the interactions between water systems, sanitation infrastructure, and public health, graduates will be prepared to develop and implement effective solutions that are both sustainable and scalable. Graduates of the MSc Program in Water, Sanitation, and Health will be equipped with a comprehensive and interdisciplinary set of competencies, enabling them to address complex challenges at the intersection of water supply, sanitation, and public health. They will possess deep theoretical knowledge, practical skills, and research abilities, preparing them for leadership roles across academia, industry, government, and non-governmental organizations. Applicants to the MSc Program in in Water, Sanitation and Health program must have BSc. degree in any of the following: Environmental Engineering, Water Resources Engineering, Civil Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Water Supply Engineering, Environmental Health, Chemistry, Biology or other related areas from a recognized university

CNCS.department_name: MSc in Water Management (with Water and Development track, Water Quality Management track, Hydrology and Water Resources Management track)

CNCS.courses: The MSc Program in Water and Wastewater Technology is designed to equip students with the interdisciplinary skills and knowledge necessary to tackle complex water management challenges. By integrating hydrological, biophysical, chemical, engineering, economic, legal, and policy-making perspectives, the program will foster a holistic approach to water and wastewater management. This MSc program equips students with in-depth knowledge and practical skills in water and wastewater treatment technologies, enabling them to design, operate, and manage sustainable systems and integrate scientific, engineering, and technological perspectives to address complex water management challenges, fostering a holistic understanding of water and wastewater systems. It creates opportunities and support students to involve in cutting-edge research that contributes to the development of innovative solutions for water and wastewater management, with a focus on the unique challenges of Ethiopia and the African region. Applicants to the MSc Program in Water and Wastewater Technology must have BSc degree in any of the following: Civil Engineering, Environmental Engineering, Water Resources Engineering, Water Supply Engineering, Sanitary Engineering, Chemical Engineering, Hydraulic Engineering, Environmental Health, Chemistry, Biology or other related areas from a recognized university.

The MSc program in Aquatic Ecosystems Management addresses the growing need for skilled professionals who can effectively manage and conserve aquatic ecosystems. This program will equip students with the knowledge, skills, and practical experience needed to tackle the complex challenges facing Ethiopia's water resources, ensuring their sustainability for future generations. The program provides advanced education and training in key areas such as aquatic ecology, water quality management, environmental impact assessment, aquatic resources and the use of modern technologies for ecosystem monitoring and conservation. The program will also emphasize the importance of integrating scientific research with practical management strategies, preparing graduates to address real-world environmental challenges. Applicants to the MSc in Aquatic Ecosystems Management program must have BSc degree in any of the following: Fisheries, Biology, Animal Sciences or other related areas from a recognized university

The MSc in Environmental Science is a two-year program that offers interdisciplinary training and innovative research across six specialized tracks: Sustainable Land and Water Resource Management, Environmental Ecology and Ecosystem Sustainability, Environmental Pollution and Remediation, Environmental Biotechnology and Sustainability, Energy and Environment, and Atmospheric and Climate Science. The program is designed to develop professionals equipped to create and apply new technologies and methodologies that address environmental issues, enhance sustainability, and contribute to the growth of a green economy in Ethiopia. It combines core courses with specialized tracks, providing students with both theoretical and practical knowledge for effective environmental management. Core courses are common to all MSc students, while specialization allows them to focus on areas aligned with their interests. To be admitted to the MSc program in Environmental Science, applicants must hold a Bachelor's degree from a recognized institution with a minimum GPA of 2.75 on a 4.0 scale or its equivalent in a relevant field, such as Chemistry, Biology, Physics, Earth Sciences, Agriculture, Forestry, Geography, Chemical Engineering, Land Resource Management, Environmental Health, or Water Resources. Applicants must submit a completed application form, official academic transcripts, and a copy of their Bachelor's degree certificate. Additionally, candidates must pass an entrance examination that assesses their knowledge and potential in environmental science and related disciplines, as well as the Graduate Admission Test (GAT). Relevant work or research experience in environmental science or a related field is an advantage

CNCS.department_name: MSc in Material Sciences and Engineering
MSc in Community Nutrition and Dietetics
CNCS.courses: The MSc program in Community Nutrition and Dietetics at Addis Ababa University, Center for Food Science and Nutrition is an intensive two-year program meticulously designed to cultivate advanced expertise in community nutrition and dietetics. This program provides a profound understanding of nutrition's pivotal role in public health, with a strong focus on applying nutritional and dietetics principles to enhance population well-being. The curriculum encompasses vital topics such as Macronutrients and Micronutrients, Medical Nutrition Therapy, Food Security, and Life Cycle Nutrition, complemented by essential soft skills in data management and scientific communication. A distinctive feature of the program is the Clinical Attachment, where students gain hands-on experience in managing chronic diseases through diet, ensuring they are well-prepared for real-world challenges. Additionally, the program places a significant emphasis on research, guiding students to engage in original research that culminates in a thesis, contributing valuable insights to the field. Admission to this prestigious program requires passing the University’s Graduate Admission Test (GAT) and holding a bachelor’s degree in a health and nutrition-related discipline. Prospective students may also need to submit a statement of purpose, participate in an interview, and pass the Center’s entrance examination. This program is ideally suited for individuals seeking to elevate their careers in public health, community nutrition, and dietetics, providing them with the comprehensive knowledge and skills necessary to address critical nutritional and public health challenges at both community and population levels.

CNCS.department_name: MSc in Food Science and Nutrition
CNCS.courses: The MSc program in Food Science and Nutrition at Addis Ababa University, Center for Food Science and Nutrition is a distinguished two-year program designed to offer advanced education in the interdisciplinary realm of food science and human nutrition. By integrating principles from chemistry, biology, technology, and nutrition, this program equips students with the expertise needed to tackle critical challenges in food quality, safety, and nutrition. The comprehensive curriculum spans key areas such as food processing technology, food chemistry, toxicology, human nutrition, and food safety management, while also providing essential training in data analysis and scientific communication. A strong emphasis on research is a cornerstone of the program, with students engaging in original research that culminates in a thesis contributing valuable insights to the field. To gain admission to this prestigious program, candidates must successfully pass the University’s Graduate Admission Test (GAT) and possess a bachelor’s degree in nutrition, horticulture, postharvest management, food technology, physical and biological sciences, chemistry, biochemistry, or a related discipline from a recognized institution. Additionally, applicants may be required to submit a statement of purpose, participate in an interview, and pass the Center’s entrance examination. This program is ideal for those aspiring to advance their careers in academia, research, industry, or government, with a focus on addressing complex issues in food and nutritional sciences.

MSc Degree in Statistics is a two-year regular program that studies the basic principles of science and their application to real-world problems. The Master of Science in Statistics offers advanced training in mathematical and applied statistics, exposure to real-world statistical applications, and specialized coursework in various areas of emphasis. This program aims to prepare students for roles as practicing statisticians, enhance research skills for those pursuing advanced degrees, and bolster the mathematical and statistical foundations of students planning for PhD studies. Graduates are equipped for careers in diverse settings like industry, government, consulting firms, and research organizations. Tailored for those seeking to cultivate strong analytical skills, the program serves as a pathway to professions in business, industry, government, non-profit work, or other quantitative fields. It offers advanced training in applied statistical theory, methodology, and techniques, bridging the gap between academia and industry. To be admitted to the M.Sc. program in a specific stream: The applicant must meet the general admission requirements of Addis Ababa University. Specific Requirements the applicant must have a bachelor's degree in statistics or any other field related to the stream from a recognized university, and the performance of the applicant in the M.Sc. entrance examination must be satisfactory as assessed and decided by the DGC. Applicants from disciplines other than Statistics who want to pursue the MSc in Statistics must present evidence that shows they took the below mentioned courses OR spend one year taking the following remedial courses with undergraduate students and score pass grades (>="C") in all courses before taking graduate level statistics courses like Introduction to Probability theory, Statistical Methods, Calculus I, Linear Algebra I, Sampling Theory, Regression Analysis, Statistical computing I, Statistical Theory of Distributions and Statistical Inference. Other stream-specific courses may also be recommended by the department graduate committee. All applicants from related fields that have scored pass grades (>="C") in all remedial courses are exempted from the department-level entrance examination

The MSc in Computational Data Science is a two-year, full-time program that integrates principles from various scientific disciplines to create a comprehensive framework for solving complex problems. This cutting-edge field combines computer science, information science, data science, and traditional disciplines such as physics, chemistry, biology, and engineering. Computational Data Science has emerged to address the growing complexity of scientific research and the need for advanced methods to manage vast datasets. Essential tools in this field include numerical computations, machine learning, high performance computing and graphical visualizations, which empower scientists to extract meaningful insights from big data, driving innovation across multiple domains. Admission requirements for the program are based on the general admission policy of the University for Master’s programs. Applicants must hold a BSc degree or equivalent in a relevant field, preferably in natural sciences, mathematics, statistics, computer science, information systems, or engineering. Other fields may also be considered appropriate by the Computational Data Science program. Applicants will be required to take an oral or written placement examination.

MEd in Science and Mathematics Education is a two-year program that covers the basics for curriculum, instructional design, general and subject-specific pedagogies, and teaching-learning of Biology, Chemistry, Physics and Mathematics. Students will receive the basics of historical and philosophical foundations, and theories and principles in teaching, learning, and assessment, and research in Biology, Chemistry, Physics and Mathematics education, including integration of technology. Admission to the program requires B.Ed., B.Sc., or B.A in either of Biology, Chemistry, Physics, or Mathematics or related fields, and applicants must fulfil all the requirements of Addis Ababa University as well as specific criteria such as GAT (Graduate Admission Test) offered by the University, and additional criteria of the Program. Examinations results as well as Degrees from foreign universities must be re-certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency. The program is offered in various modalities – regular, extension, summer, weekends, or special arrangements like distance or special self-paced learning programs.

CNCS.department_name: MSc in Chemistry (Analytical, Organic, Inorganic and Physical)

MSc in Physics (Laser Spectroscopy, Space Physics, Polymer Physics, Atmospheric Physics, Condensed Matter Physics, Astronomy and Astrophysics, Nuclear Physics, Statistical Physics and Modelling, and Quantum Optics and Information)

CNCS.courses: MSc in Computer Science is a Two year regular program that studies advanced concepts, theories, and practical applications in the field of computer science. The program aims to develop highly skilled professionals and researchers who are prepared to lead and innovate in the rapidly evolving field of computer science in Software Engineering, Data and Web Engineering and Network and Security. The program enable students often engage in research /thesis, where they apply their knowledge to solve real-world problems or contribute to the advancement of the field. Admission requirement for the program is based on entrance exam and/or other criteria set by the Department.) , the choice of the track for the specializations after completing the common modules in the first semester, will choose and join one of the specializations as per the direction given by the DGC

CNCS.department_name: MSc in Geophysics (with tracks; Applied Geophysics and Solid Earth Geophysics

CBE.department_name: Bachelor of Arts in Business Administration and Information Systems
CBE.courses: The BA in Business Administration and Information Systems (BAIS) is a four-year regular program that combines core business principles with cutting-edge information technology skills. This interdisciplinary program equips students with a strong foundation in business administration, including management, marketing, finance, logistics, strategic management, and project management, while developing their proficiency in information systems, data analysis, and application often IT in business. Students will gain practical knowledge of how to leverage information technology to solve business problems, improve operational efficiency, and drive innovation at the workplace. The program begins with introductory courses in both business and information systems, where students learn the fundamentals concepts, business ethics, and computer programming for application in business. As they progress, students engage in more specialized coursework that includes database management, systems analysis and design, e-commerce, and digital business strategies. This comprehensive approach prepares graduates to meet the demands of the modern business world, where technology and business processes are increasingly intertwined. Admission to the program is based on the results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student, and the capacity of the BAIS Departments and School of Commerce. Additionally, students must pass the Undergraduate Admission Test (UGAT) to be offered by the University. For international students, foreign examination results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE. Graduates of the BA in Business Administration and Information Systems program will be well prepared to pursue careers in various sectors, including corporate business, Small and medium businesses, government enterprises, and non-governmental organizations. They can also join the world of entrepreneurship and business consulting sector or continue their education in available graduate studies.

CBE.department_name: Bachelor of Arts in Logistics and Supply Chain Management
CBE.courses: The Bachelor of Arts (BA) in Logistics and Supply Chain Management is a comprehensive four year program that equips students with essential knowledge and skills to excel with a deep understanding of logistics principles, supply chain dynamics, and operations management. They are skilled in data analysis, problem-solving, and project management, with practical experience gained through internships and case studies. They are well-prepared for roles such as Logistics Coordinator, Supply Chain Analyst, or Operations Manager, Business Logistics specialist, Humanitarian Logistics specialist, Procurement Specialist, Inventory Manager, Transportation Manager, Warehouse Manager, Supply Chain Manager, Operations Manager, Customer Service Manager (Logistics Focus), Consultant (Logistics and Supply Chain) and Supply chain sustainability specialist, Freight Forwarder, Transistor and Customer clearing officer in public, business and humanitarian organization. The BA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management is designed to provide students with a thorough understanding of logistics principles, supply chain management strategies, and emerging technologies. The curriculum covers crucial areas such as (a) Logistics Services Management (Warehouse Management, Transportation Management, Port and Terminal Operations Management, Customs Clearing & Freight Forwarding Management, Distribution Logistics Management), (b) International Issues in Logistics and Supply Chain Management (Humanitarian Logistics Management, Sustainable Supply Chain Management, Global Supply Chain Management), (c) Strategic Sourcing and Procurement Management (Negotiation & Contract Management, Strategic Sourcing and Supplier Relationships Management, Government Procurement, Foreign Procurement), (d) Supply Chain Operations and Strategy ( Ecommerce and Supply Chain Information System, Strategic Supply Chain Management and Essentials of Supply Chain Management)among others. Add to this, students will gain practical experience through internships, co-op programs, and industry-sponsored projects, applying theoretical knowledge to real-world challenges. The program also emphasizes the integration of new technologies like digital platforms, automation, and data analytics to enhance supply chain efficiency. Additionally, students will address regulatory, environmental, and ethical considerations in logistics, with a special focus on the logistical landscape of Ethiopia and East Africa.

CBE.department_name: Bachelor of Arts in Public Administration & Development
CBE.courses: With its long history as one of oldest fields of study in Ethiopia, the BA in Public Administration and Development Management (PADM) is a four-year program that focuses on the study of themes falling within the wider public domain such as public policy, governance, public finance management, human resource administration, development management, etc. The students joining the program will be acquainted with applicable theories, practices, and methods of public administration. The program requires students to explore and comprehend dynamic issues in real life contexts through case studies and exposes them to the practical world through apprenticeship programs. As a multidisciplinary field of inquiry, obviously, the program incorporates relevant courses being offered by other departments and academic units of AAU. Ultimately, up on concluding their degree, graduates of PADM will be competent professionals well-equipped with the pertinent knowledge and skills required to take-up expert and leadership positions among public, private, and non-governmental organizations. Admission requirements for the program is based on the results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the student’s choice/interest, the capacity of department, as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Results of foreign examinations must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE.

CBE.department_name: Bachelor of Arts in Marketing Management
CBE.courses: The BA in Marketing Management program is a Four-year regular program, designed to Equip graduates to Fit to the most demanded skill of today’s marketing Environment; Not Just selling a product but build and maintain successful long-lasting relationship with customers. Thus, the Program incorporate courses from basic theories of marketing, Sales Management, brand Management, marketing Information system, E-Marketing, Marketing analytics and strategic Marketing Management to name some, which will give our graduates an in-depth understanding of the competition and the consumer in return makes them to Implement marketing tactic successfully. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Canters as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their Equivalency to the EHEECE.

The Bachelor of Arts in Management program equips future business leaders and entrepreneurs with a strong foundation in management principles and practical skills. Students gain expertise in key areas such as Management Science, Marketing, Human Resources, Strategic Management, and Project Management, alongside foundational knowledge in Accounting and Economics. The program admits both regular and evening students, with entry requirements aligned with the standards set by the Ministry of Education (MoE) and the 2024 Senate Legislation of Addis Ababa University (AAU). Spanning four years for regular students and five years for evening students, the program is taught by expert faculty, offering personalized mentorship and real-world business insights. Graduates are prepared to make informed decisions, manage resources, lead organizations, and apply management concepts, principles, and best practices in various contexts. For more information, visit the AAU website or contact the Department of Management or Registrar’s office at +251111229611, +251111229378, or management@aau.edu.et.

The BA in Economics program is four and five - years program for regular and extension students respectively with a comprehensive curriculum that covers a wide range of topics within the field. It incorporates Foundational Courses; such as Microeconomics and Macroeconomics, Computational Courses with emphasis on theoretical frameworks and Economic models, Statistics, Mathematics. Econometrics and statistical software, Sectoral Courses; Financial Economics. Monetary Economics. International Economics. Public Finance. Industrial Economics. Agricultural Economics. Labor Economics. Development Economics, Critical Thinking Courses: such as the History of Economic Thought to foster analytical skills, and Writing skills with language proficiency. Graduates of a BA in Economics have a variety of career opportunities across different sectors. The common paths of the graduates include: Government and Public Sector, Economic Analyst, Economic Policy Advisor, Researcher for governmental and non-government agencies These career paths leverage the theoretical knowledge and practical skills developed during the program, preparing graduates for diverse roles in the economy. Graduates are well-equipped to

CBE.department_name: Bachelor of Arts in Accounting and Finance
CBE.courses: The BA in Accounting and Finance program is a four-year regular program (or five-years evening program) that endeavors to provide a cohesive, dynamic, forward-looking, tech-driven, and marketdriven educational experiences for the emergence of scholars, leaders, thinkers, and other contributors to the society. Upon graduation from the program, the graduate from Bachelor of Arts (BA) Degree in Accounting and Finance should have the requisite competencies constituting knowledge, skills and attitudes in accounting and finance. Anyone who wishes to join the Bachelor of Arts Degree Program in Accounting and Finance is required to meet the entry requirements of higher institutions stated by Ministry of Education (MoE) and in the Senate legislation of Addis Ababa University (AAU).

Master of Business Information Systems (MBIS) is a two-year regular program that studies the basic theories and practices of business core functions and information technology and Systems. Students will dive deep to the broader and advanced disciplines, which include organizational behavior, operations management, strategic management, advanced business analytics and decision support system, digital business and technology, IT Service Management, information system security and governance, innovation and technology management, project management and financial and managerial accounting. MBIS graduates may find work as Business Analysts, Systems Analysts, Project Leaders, IT Managers, Business Managers, Information Management Specialists, Information Resource Managers, Enterprise Systems Managers, and Business consultants. MBIS is a degree that provides a broad technical and managerial understanding of business information systems, including database, systems design, and IS strategy. The broad range of courses from strategic management to information system security makes the MBIS an extremely comprehensive degree and this will contribute to students' career versatility ranging from system specialists to business managers Applicants for MBIS program are required to have Bachelor's degree from recognized university or college and are required to pass Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE). Applicants may further need to pass department level exams

Master of Art in Project Management is a two-year generic program designed to provide a profound knowledge and expertise in project management for those seeking such competencies and aspiring to apply the same in their respective areas of engagement (industry, commerce and public services). The courses constituting the program contain a mix of managerial and technical skills as well as cover both hard and soft skills courses of Project Management Body Knowledge Areas. The program enables students to build broad based skills and knowledge in project management that will give them excellent opportunities to their immediate and long term careers. It is designed to help students develop expertise of designing projects, crafting objectives for them, drawing up strategies for getting them done, and putting them into effect for better project outcomes. To get admitted to the program, applicants must have a first degree from a recognized university in any field of study, a minimum CGPA as per AAU’s senate legislation, successfully pass GAT examination in alignment with AAU Senate Legislation, and complete a personal assessment form. Applicants who fulfill the above criteria will sit for a program level exam to be prepared by the school for final admission. Admission of applicants to the program whose written exam scores are competitive will be determined based on their scores and the available space (capacity). When ties in scores are encountered, priority will be given to female applicants.

Master of Business Leadership (MBL) program is a two year regular program designed to develop advanced leadership skills and strategic thinking for professionals aiming to excel in executive and senior management roles. The program typically includes coursework in organizational behavior, strategic management, financial analysis, and change management. It often emphasizes practical, real-world applications through case studies, projects, and internships. The MBL aims to equip students with the competencies needed to lead effectively, drive organizational success, and navigate complex business environments. Applicants for MBL program need to have Bachelor Degree from a recognized university or college. They are also required to pass Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE). Applicants may further need to pass department level exams incase number of applicants exceeds capacity

CBE.department_name: MA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management
CBE.courses: The Master of Arts (MA) in Logistics and Supply Chain Management at Addis Ababa University is a prestigious graduate program designed to address the evolving needs of the global logistics and supply chain sector. Offered by the Department of Logistics and Supply Chain Management within the College of Business and Economics, this program equips students with advanced knowledge and skills for managing complex logistics and supply chain systems. Key Areas of Study: Logistics Functions: Transportation (On-Time Delivery Rate, Transportation Costs), Warehousing (Warehouse Efficiency, Inventory Accuracy), Inventory Management (Inventory Turnover Ratio, Stockout Rate), Order Fulfillment (Order Accuracy, Fulfillment Cycle Time), Demand Planning (Forecast Accuracy, Inventory Levels), Procurement (Supplier Lead Time, Cost Savings), Logistics Network Design (Cost Efficiency, Network Optimization), Packaging (Packaging Accuracy, Damage Rate), Returns Management (Return Rate, Return Processing Time), Customs and Compliance (Compliance Rate, Customs Clearance Time), Supply Chain Coordination (Partner Collaboration, Coordination Efficiency), and Information Management (Data Accuracy, System Uptime). Supply Chain Management Process: Planning (Demand Forecast Accuracy, Inventory Turnover Ratio), Sourcing (Supplier Lead Time, Cost Savings), Manufacturing (Production Cycle Time, First Pass Yield), Logistics (On-Time Delivery Rate, Warehouse Efficiency), Distribution (Order Accuracy, Delivery Lead Time), Returns Management (Return Rate, Return Processing Time), Customer Service (Customer Satisfaction Score, Order Status Inquiry Response Time), Performance Measurement (Overall Equipment Effectiveness, Cost per Order), Information Flow (Data Accuracy, System Uptime), and Strategic Management (Supply Chain Resilience, Strategic Alignment). The curriculum emphasizes strategic management, digital integration, and operational efficiency, incorporating Industry 4.0 technologies such as artificial intelligence, blockchain, and the Internet of Things (IoT). Students gain expertise in strategic supply chain management, digital technologies, project management, leadership, logistics operations, and humanitarian logistics. The MA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management offers a comprehensive education that spans several essential areas. In strategic supply chain management, students gain expertise in creating and implementing strategies to enhance supply chain performance, adaptability, and alignment with organizational goals. This includes advanced scenario planning and strategic decision-making. The program emphasizes digital integration and data analysis, training students to leverage digital technologies and analyze large datasets to drive efficiency and innovation. This focus on modern technologies prepares students to integrate these tools effectively into supply chain operations. Students also develop proficiency in project management , learning to plan, execute, and oversee complex supply chain projects while managing resources, timelines, and budgets. The program fosters leadership and team management skills, preparing graduates to lead projects, motivate teams, and resolve conflicts in various supply chain contexts. In logistics and operations management, students acquire in-depth knowledge of logistics operations, such as customs clearance, freight forwarding, and supply chain modeling, enhancing their ability to manage and optimize logistics processes. Additionally, the program includes a focus on humanitarian logistics, equipping students to manage logistics operations in crisis situations and support effective disaster response and recovery efforts. Graduates of the MA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management will be well-prepared for leadership roles in diverse sectors, including manufacturing, transportation, retail, and humanitarian organizations. They will possess the skills necessary to address complex supply chain challenges, drive strategic improvements, and enhance the efficiency and competitiveness of both global and local supply chains. MA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management is designed to accommodate both national and international students, the program offers flexible study options, including regular, evening, and tutor-assisted distance learning formats. It aligns with Addis Ababa University's mission to provide transformative education and address the growing demand for skilled logistics and supply chain professionals. Globally, logistics is vital for economic growth and efficiency, with substantial investments and rapid expansion anticipated. In Ethiopia, the logistics sector is crucial for enhancing national competitiveness and addressing challenges associated with being a landlocked country. Despite significant investments, Ethiopia faces challenges in logistics performance, which this program aims to address by preparing skilled professionals to drive improvements and contribute to national development goals. The program’s mission is to cultivate professionals with advanced strategic thinking, leadership, and problem-solving skills, emphasizing ethical practices and sustainability. The curriculum integrates hands-on learning with industry partnerships to bridge the gap between theory and practice. Students will gain expertise in strategic supply chain management, data analysis, digital technologies, project management, and leadership, among other areas. The curriculum addresses a broad spectrum of sector-specific and industry-specific issues in supply chain management. In agricultural supply chain management, it covers the entire farm to-fork process, including the logistics of perishable goods and the supply of inputs such as seeds and fertilizers. For health and pharmaceutical supply chain management, the program delves into the intricacies of drug and pharmaceutical supply chains, medical equipment and devices, patient flow management, and the complexities of regulatory compliance. In retail supply chain management, the focus is on omni-channel fulfilment, integrating physical and online retail operations, as well as managing inventory, collaborating with suppliers, and enhancing the customer experience. The curriculum also explores manufacturing supply chain management, addressing key concepts such as just-in-time production, lean manufacturing practices, global sourcing, and quality control. The program includes energy supply chain managementsupply chain management, which involves resource extraction, energy distribution, infrastructure management, and a commitment to sustainability. For the automotive industrysupply chain management, the curriculum covers parts procurement, assembly line coordination, supplier quality management, and aftermarket parts distribution. In the aerospace industrysupply chain management, students learn about component manufacturing, regulatory compliance, logistics and maintenance, and risk management. Food and beverage supply chain management is addressed through topics like production scheduling, cold chain management to ensure product freshness, supplier quality assurance, and traceability. Finally, the curriculum covers construction supply chain management, focusing on materials procurement, project coordination, subcontractor management, and compliance with safety regulations. Additionally the program captures case specific issues on topics including fast-moving consumer goods (FMCG), value chain management, humanitarian logistics, service supply chain management, cold chain management, transport management, procurement, reverse logistics, and supply chain digitalization. The program is substantiated with a wide range of technologies and solutions, including ERP systems, SCM software, WMS, TMS, RFID, GPS, blockchain, big data analytics, cloud computing, Electronic Health Records (EHR) Systems, Pharmacy Management Systems, Hospital Information Systems (HIS), Inventory Management Systems, Barcode and RFID Technology, Track and Trace Systems, Cold Chain Monitoring Solutions, Agricultural extension digital hub and digital map supply management, Telemedicine Platforms, Data Analytics and Business Intelligence, Predictive Analytics for Demand Forecasting, Supply Chain Visibility Platforms, HMIS, LMIS, Precision Agriculture Tools (e.g., GPS, GIS), Satellite Imaging and Remote Sensing, Farm-to-Fork Tracking Systems, Agricultural Machinery Telematics, Electronic Marketplaces and Platforms, Smart Packaging Solutions, Machine Learning for Demand Forecasting, Digital Twins for Simulation and Optimization, Automated Sorting and Grading Systems, IPLS, Automated Dispensing Systems, Robotic Process Automation (RPA), Artificial Intelligence (AI) for Inventory Optimization, Cloud-Based Supply Chain Management Solutions, Internet of Things (IoT) for Real-Time Monitoring, Electronic Data Interchange (EDI) Systems, Digital Twins for Simulation and Planning, Smart Sensors for Environmental Monitoring, APS systems, RPA, AR, drones, 3D printing, EDI, smart sensors, Cold chain management, predictive analytics, digital twins, autonomous vehicles, CRM systems, inventory optimization tools, collaboration platforms, and simulation software. This comprehensive curriculum ensures that graduates are equipped with the knowledge and skills to address the diverse and evolving challenges in logistics and supply chain management.

CBE.department_name: MSc in Public Management and Policy
CBE.courses: Masters of Art Degree in Public Policy and Development management PPDM is a two year program established with the aim to train qualified professional policy analysts, competent managers who will able to assume key positions in government and non-government organizations. The program will acquaint students with the state-of-the-art knowledge, analytical skills and qualities in problem solving, decision-making, and development planning and programming needed by both government non-government organizations in Ethiopia. The admission procedure should strictly observe the requirements, policies, procedures, rules and regulations of Addis Ababa University designed for graduate programs.

CBE.department_name: MA in Marketing Management (Marketing Management Track and Digital Marketing and E-commerce Track)
CBE.courses: The Master of Arts in Marketing Management is an extensive graduate program designed for individuals looking to deepen their knowledge and skills in the rapidly evolving field of modern marketing. The MA in Marketing Management provides a strong foundation in key marketing concepts, such as consumer behavior, strategic planning, market research, branding, and communication. The curriculum is crafted to enhance analytical thinking and foster creativity, ensuring that graduates are well-equipped to design and execute effective marketing strategies across various industries. With a focus on both theory and practical application, the program incorporates real-world case studies, industry projects, and experiential learning opportunities, preparing students to take on leadership roles within the marketing sector. Additionally, the program offers a specialized track in Digital Marketing and E-Commerce, focusing on the digital components of marketing to meet the demands of today’s technology-driven economy. This track explores the strategies, tools, and techniques crucial for success in digital marketing and e-commerce, covering topics such as search engine optimization (SEO), social media marketing, digital advertising, content marketing, email marketing, web analytics, and e-commerce platforms.

CSSAH.department_name: BA in Journalism and Communication (Broadcast Journalism) with Journalism and Communication (Multimedia)
CSSAH.courses: BA in Broadcast and Multimedia Journalism The BA in Broadcast and Multimedia Journalism (BMMJ) is a four-year study program that intends to prepare graduates to work for both broadcast (radio and television) and multimedia (digital and multimodal) platforms. In addition to broadcast and multimedia production skill courses, students take several crosscutting courses related to history, theories, technologies, ethics/law/policy, politics, internship and research in mass communication. For successful admission students are required to: Complete secondary school education with satisfactory results from social and natural sciences background. Have a generally positive interest and motivation to learn and work in the field of journalism and communication. A good level of English language comprehension that would enable their understanding of the courses. Be willing to attend any entrance exam that the school may have to prepare. Meet any general admission requirements of Addis Ababa university and Ministry of education including their successfully completion of freshman program courses

CSSAH.department_name: BA in Public Relations and Strategic Communication
CSSAH.courses: BA in Public Relations and Strategic Communication The BA in Public Relations and Strategic Communication (PRSC) is a four-year study program that intends to prepare graduates to work as communication professionals and applied communicators for all types of organizations and sectors. In addition to mainstream PR and strategic communication courses, students take helpful courses related to advertising, marketing, media, politics in connection with the field, also internship and research in PR and strategic communication. For successful admission students are required to: Complete secondary school education with satisfactory results from social and natural sciences background. Have a generally positive interest and motivation to learn and work in the field of pR and strategic communication. A good level of English language comprehension that would enable their understanding of the courses. Be willing to attend any entrance exam that the school may have to prepare. Meet any general admission requirements of Addis Ababa university and Ministry of education including their successfully completion of freshman program courses

CSSAH.department_name: BFA in Fine Arts and Design

CSSAH.courses: Yared School of Music (YSM) offers a comprehensive curriculum that balances traditional and contemporary music practices. It includes a general music performance stream and a specialized jazz stream, allowing students to explore a wide range of musical styles, including traditional Ethiopian music, jazz, and popular music. This approach equips students with the versatility needed for diverse careers in the music industry. The program is designed for undergraduate students pursuing a Bachelor of Arts degree in music. The curriculum provides a solid theoretical foundation and practical skills essential for success in various music-related fields. By emphasizing competency-based learning, we ensure that our graduates are well prepared to meet the demands of an evolving music landscape in Ethiopia and beyond. Yared School of Music aligns with Addis Ababa University’s mission to foster an inclusive and innovative educational environment that nurtures talent and contributes to societal development. The YSM commitment to high-quality music education, state-of-the-art instruments, and skilled educators enhances its capacity to produce graduates who significantly contribute to Ethiopia's cultural, intellectual, and socio economic growth.

CSSAH.department_name: BA in Theater Arts with Film Studies
CSSAH.courses: The Bachelor of Arts in Theater and Film Arts provides a comprehensive education that blends theoretical knowledge with practical skills and prepares students for a variety of careers in the performing and cinematic arts. The program emphasizes a solid foundation in the principles of theater and film production, while fostering creativity, critical analysis, and collaboration through hands-on projects and coursework. Students take core courses such as acting techniques, directing for stage and screen, and playwriting, as well as film editing, graphic design, and digital marketing. They demonstrate their skills in a final project in their senior year, culminating in a public presentation. Practical experiences, including workshops, internships and student productions, deepen their learning. Graduates are well equipped for careers in acting, production, screenwriting, media, printmaking and arts management, and will be well-rounded professionals who can contribute to the dynamic fields of theater and film. Admission requirements for the program are based on the results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the student's choice and the capacity of the department/school/center and the Undergraduate Admission Test (UGAT) offered by the University. The Ministry of Education for equivalence with the EHEECE must check foreign examination results.

CSSAH.department_name: BA in Political Science & International Relations
CSSAH.courses: The BA in Political and Science and International Relations is a four-year regular and evening program. The program aims at training early-career professionals in the fields of Political Science and International Relations. The graduates will be proficient in the field of study in terms of grasping key theoretical knowledge, methodologies and methods in the field of study, develop strong analytical skills, and acquire demonstrable skills in writing and presenting studies and reports in academic context as well as other levels. From the professional point of view, the Political Science and International Relations undergraduate program provides education and training catering to the needs of public as well as private institutions. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE.

CSSAH.department_name: BA in History and Heritage Studies
CSSAH.courses: The BA program in History and Heritage Studies is a four-year regular program that studies history covering different regions, themes and periods and the practice of history as a discipline; the theoretical and practical approaches to heritage studies and basic theoretical schools and field methods in archaeology. In the context of history, the program gives emphasis to major political, economic, social, cultural, etc. developments in Ethiopia and the Horn from earliest times to the present, while heritage studies puts accent on theories and approaches to heritage studies and their application to conserving heritages, promoting and developing heritage tourism in Ethiopia & the Horn. The History and Heritage Studies B.A. program provides students the opportunity to combine (integrate) three disciplines namely history, archaeology, and heritage studies (heritage conservation, museum and collection management) thereby acquiring foundational (basic) knowledge and a wide-range practical skills in these fields. In effect, the Program allows students to make sense of the past and its link with the present, and possibly the future. Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of Departments/Schools/Centers as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Results of foreign examinations must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE.

The Bachelor of Social Work (BSW) is a four years undergraduate degree regular program (and five years in the continuing/extension program). Social Work being an academic discipline and a practice based profession is concerned with meeting the basic and complex needs of individuals, families, groups and communities and enhance human wellbeing. Students will hence learn about the strengths and challenges of a diverse population in a changing and evolving global community and will develop their understanding of human development, behavior and the social, economic and cultural institutions and interactions. The program will also expose students to critical practice knowledge; skills and the essential values of the profession including various approaches to interventions, ethical considerations, and the importance of cultural competence in working with diverse populations. It is a stepping stone for those who aspire to make a difference in society by helping individuals, families, and communities and overcoming challenges and improve their quality of life. The program strives to prepare tomorrow’s social practitioners to effect social change and well-being for all people. Admission requirement for the program is based on good results in the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of the School as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign students’ examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE

CSSAH.department_name: BA in Geography and Development Studies
CSSAH.courses: The BEd in Geography is a four-year regular undergraduate Program aimed at preparing prospective teachers of geography for Ethiopian schools and beyond. The Program enables prospective teachers to acquire knowledge in broad areas of geography and geography education; develop 21st century skills related to geography and geography education; and develop a positive attitude towards teaching geography; and the teaching profession, in general. The graduates of this Program will also have an opportunity to serve in such areas as curriculum development; consultancy; environmental and sustainability education; and related services. More importantly, the graduates make a significant contribution towards greening of the physical environment as well as human mind to make the world a better place for ALL beings, human and non-human! Admission requirement for the program is based on results of the Ethiopian Higher Education Entrance Certificate Examination (EHEECE), the choice of the student and the capacity of the Departments of Social Science and Language Education as well as UGAT (Undergraduate Admission Test) offered by the University. Foreign examinations results must be certified by the Ministry of Education for their equivalency to the EHEECE

CSSAH.department_name: Masters in Constitutional and Federalism Studies
CSSAH.courses: Masters in Constitutional and Federalism Studies (LL.M and MA Tracks) The Masters in Constitutional and Federalism Studies is a one-and-a-half-year regular program (three years in the extension program) that studies diverse areas of Constitutional and Federalism issues to build the knowledge, research, consultancy and teaching capabilities of the students in current Constitutional and Federalism matters. Students who have first degree in Law (LLB) or BA degree in political science, economics, civics and ethics, anthropology, sociology, history, development studies, public administration, and other related fields of study who join the program will be engaged in in-depth studies of Constitutional and Federalism issues to conduct quality research from an interdisciplinary and comparative perspective and create research-based knowledge on the constitutional and federal order that Ethiopia follows. The courses to be covered include: Research Methods in Law and Social Science; Constitutional and Federalism Theories; Comparative Constitutional Law; State Constitutions and Local Government; Fiscal Federalism; Constitutional Interpretation and Adjudication; Intergovernmental Relations and Public Policy; elective courses (LL.M and MA Track); and Master’s Thesis (LL.M and MA Track). In the LL.M Track, the elective courses will be chosen from the following: Ethiopian Constitutional Development; Advanced Administrative Law; or African Constitutional Theory & Practice. In the MA Track, the elective courses will be chosen from the following: Federalism and Management of Diversity; Devolution and Regional Integration in the Horn; or Federalism, Human Rights and Gender. Admission to the LL.M Track is limited to applicants who have first degree in law (LLB). Admission to the MA Track is open to applicants who have LLB degree or BA degree in political science, economics, civics and ethics, anthropology, sociology, history, development studies, public administration, and other related fields of study. Admission to the Program (both tracks) is based on Graduate Admission Test (GAT) of the Addis Ababa University and admission test to be administered jointly by the School of Law and the Centre for Federalism and Governance Studies

CSSAH.department_name: Masters in Human Rights (with tracks LLM/MA)
CSSAH.courses: The Masters in Human Rights Studies (LL.M and MA Tracks) is a one-and-a-half-year regular program (three years in the extension program) that studies diverse areas of Human Rights issues to build the knowledge, research, consultancy and teaching capabilities of the students in Human Rights. Students who have first degree in Law (LLB) or BA degree in fields relevant to human rights such as political science, sociology, philosophy, history, economics, development studies and other fields of social sciences and humanities who join the program will be engaged in in-depth studies of human rights issues to conduct quality research from an interdisciplinary and comparative perspective; create research-based knowledge on human rights; and contribute towards the realization of human rights. The common courses to the Tracks include: Research Methods in Law and Social Science; History, philosophy and politics of human rights; Culture, Social Change and Human Rights; Global and African Regional Human Rights systems; Ethiopian Human Rights System; and Human Rights, Economic Thoughts and Development. The additional courses to be covered in the LL.M Track include: International Humanitarian Law; International Criminal Law; Socio-Economic Rights; two elective courses; and LL.M Thesis. The two elective courses will be chosen from the following: Rights of Migrants, Refugees and IDPs; Child Rights; Rights of Minorities and Indigenous Peoples; Rights of Persons with Disabilities; Human Rights Monitoring, Investigation and Advocacy

CSSAH.department_name: MA in Public Relations and Strategic Communication
CSSAH.courses: MA in Public Relations and Strategic Communication (PRSC) The MA in Public Relations and Strategic Communication (PRSC) is a two-year graduate program that intends to prepare graduates for advanced-level effective contribution in the general field of public relations and strategic communication, including teaching and research in the field. Students are set to attend compulsory advanced-level PR and strategic communication courses as well as a thesis work in their field. For successful admission, students are required to: complete their first-degree education with satisfactory results from social and natural sciences backgrounds. have a generally positive interest and motivation to learn and contribute to the field of multimedia journalism and communication. have an advanced level of English language comprehension that would enable their understanding of course works and also to successfully undertake their MA thesis. be willing to attend any entrance exam that the school may have to prepare. meet any general admission requirements of Addis Ababa University and the Ministry of Education, including a successful pass mark in the AAU’s Graduate Admission Test (GAT).

CSSAH.department_name: MA in Journalism and Communication with a specialty Broadcast Journalism
MA in Journalism and Communication with a specialty Multimedia Journalism
CSSAH.courses: MA in Journalism and Communication (Multimedia Journalism) The MA in Journalism and Communication (Multimedia Journalism) is a two-year graduate program that intends to prepare graduates for advanced-level effective contribution in multimedia (digital and multimodal) journalism, communication, teaching, and research. Students have the chance to attend advanced-level multimedia and communication courses as well as thesis work in their field. For successful admission, students are required to: complete their first-degree education with satisfactory (a minimum CGPA of 2.00) results from social and natural sciences backgrounds. have a generally positive interest and motivation to learn and contribute to the field of multimedia journalism and communication. have an advanced level of English language comprehension that would enable their understanding of course works and also to successfully undertake their MA thesis. be willing to attend any entrance exam that the school may have to prepare. meet any general admission requirements of Addis Ababa University and the Ministry of Education, including a successful pass mark in the AAU’s Graduate Admission Test (GAT).
